# System Thesis

## Canonical Objective

Define a deterministic, AI-native structure for factual memory where paraphrases converge to the same semantic identity without collapsing near-neighbor facts.

Working name: Deterministic Semantic Fact Graph (`DSFG`).

## Iteration Scope

Iteration 1 fixed semantic identity semantics (Design Target 1).
Iteration 2 formalized relation edge algebra for dependency, derivation, contradiction, and evidence support (Design Target 2), with deterministic insertion/update semantics.
Iteration 3 formalizes dual-time revision semantics, deterministic supersession/rollback, and as-of query behavior (Design Target 3 + part of 5).
Iteration 4 formalizes provenance and uncertainty calculus with deterministic confidence composition and lineage explainability (Design Target 4 + part of 5).
Iteration 5 formalizes distributed sync convergence with replica-stable merge semantics, conflict classes, and deterministic resolution precedence (Design Target 6 + part of 5).
Iteration 6 formalizes deterministic interval surgery for partial valid-time overlaps with rollback-safe projection semantics (Design Target 3 + 5 + 6 closure on overlap behavior).
Iteration 7 formalizes deterministic cross-epoch migration transactions that convert quarantined operations into admitted canonical state without violating replay/convergence invariants (Design Target 6 + 5 closure on epoch cutover behavior).
Iteration 8 formalizes deterministic witness-independence qualifiers for same-rule inference witnesses, reducing conservative undercount while preserving anti-inflation guarantees and replay stability (Design Target 4 + 5 closure on intra-rule independence handling).
Iteration 9 formalizes deterministic multi-hop migration governance with path composition, cutover precedence, and non-destructive compaction while preserving replay/convergence guarantees (Design Target 6 + 5 closure on epoch-path governance).
Iteration 10 formalizes deterministic interval-surgery strategy governance with policy sequencing, admissibility constraints, and cutover-stable overlap arbitration (Design Target 3 + 5 + 6 closure on surgery strategy governance).
Iteration 11 formalizes deterministic physical retention and garbage-collection governance for compacted migration versions and superseded interval-surgery projections while preserving replay/audit determinism (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on retention control).
Iteration 12 formalizes deterministic bridge-safe same-rule witness aggregation using anchor-mass decomposition, reducing transitive bridge undercount without confidence inflation or replay drift (Design Target 4 + 5 closure on bridge-coupled witness handling).
Iteration 13 formalizes deterministic retention governance expansion to canonical base-record payload classes via immutable semantic-kernel boundaries and hash-addressed base payload capsules (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on base-retention replay safety).
Iteration 14 formalizes deterministic capsule caching and eviction governance with policy-sequenced cache leases and pin-safe eviction constraints, adding bounded-latency rehydration contracts for reclaimed base payloads without weakening replay/query invariance (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on retention cache governance).
Iteration 15 formalizes deterministic multi-codec capsule profile governance with codec/key cutover sequencing, rotation confluence checks, and compatibility-retirement guards while preserving logical replay/query invariance (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on codec/key governance).
Iteration 16 formalizes deterministic workload-tiered cache/profile policy families with explicit tier admissibility, precedence, and bounded-cost placement semantics while preserving logical replay/query invariance (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on workload-tier governance).
Iteration 17 formalizes deterministic telemetry-backed tier utility governance with anti-gaming admission semantics, utility confluence protections, and replay-stable utility cutovers while preserving logical query invariance (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on telemetry-calibrated tier utility).
Iteration 18 formalizes deterministic cross-artifact-class global budget arbitration with fair-share and SLA-floor allocation semantics that compose with telemetry-backed utility and preserve logical replay/query invariance (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on global budget arbitration).
Iteration 19 formalizes deterministic long-horizon global budget memory with deficit carryover, burst-credit decay, and SLA-debt rollback semantics so cross-window arbitration remains replay-stable under rollback and policy churn (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on horizon budget memory).
Iteration 20 formalizes deterministic utilization attestation and late-window reconciliation for global budget memory inputs so carryover/credit/debt transitions are tamper-evident and replay-stable under delayed telemetry arrival (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on utilization attestation governance).
Iteration 21 formalizes deterministic attester-set rotation and trust-tiered quorum cutover semantics with historical proof continuity so utilization-attested memory/arbitration transitions remain replay-stable across trust roster/key churn (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on attester trust governance).
Iteration 22 formalizes deterministic attester-accountability memory with slashing, reputation decay, and reinstatement semantics so trust-tiered utilization/memory/arbitration inputs remain replay-stable under adversarial fault evidence and rollback (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on attester accountability governance).
Iteration 23 formalizes deterministic cross-domain attester fault adjudication and appeal-finality semantics so accountability transitions consume only finalized adjudication lineage and remain replay-stable under disputed evidence and rollback (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on attester adjudication governance).
Iteration 24 formalizes deterministic cross-domain adjudication portability semantics with jurisdiction-weight bundles, evidence-envelope normalization, and appeal-review multiplexing so heterogeneous dispute inputs remain replay-stable before adjudication/finality consumption (Design Target 5 + 6 closure on adjudication portability governance).
Iteration 25 formalizes deterministic privacy-preserving portability disclosure semantics with redaction commitments, selective evidence reveal proofs, and replay-stable review attestations so cross-domain portability remains verifiable without exposing full evidence payloads (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on privacy-preserving portability disclosure governance).
Iteration 26 formalizes deterministic portability-disclosure lifecycle semantics with privacy-budget replenishment, disclosure revocation, and commitment-scheme agility so reveal authority can be replenished/revoked/rotated without replay drift (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle governance).
Iteration 27 formalizes deterministic multi-profile lifecycle calibration semantics with adaptive replenishment envelopes, revocation granularity classes, and commitment compatibility matrices so lifecycle profile selection remains replicated and replay-stable under heterogeneous dispute pressure (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle calibration governance).
Iteration 28 formalizes deterministic calibration-selector objective governance with replicated privacy/fairness/cost weights and hysteresis family cutovers so calibration family choice cannot drift by runtime-local scorer state (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle objective governance).
Iteration 29 formalizes deterministic lifecycle-objective signal integrity governance with canonical evidence attestations, lag-compensated normalization lineage, and anti-manipulation verdict semantics so objective inputs cannot drift by runtime-local telemetry adapters (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal integrity governance).
Iteration 30 formalizes deterministic cross-domain objective-signal federation governance with replicated federation attestations, observer-diversity escrow, and stale-feed quarantine lineage so cross-domain objective inputs cannot drift by runtime-local federation adapters (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation governance).
Iteration 31 formalizes deterministic federation rehabilitation governance with replicated quarantine-release proofs, escrow-unlock fairness lineage, and cross-domain clock-skew compensation records so federation recovery cannot drift by runtime-local remediation adapters (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation governance).
Iteration 32 formalizes deterministic federation rehabilitation objective coupling governance with replicated selector clamps, fairness-preserving unlock backpressure, and skew-cutover hysteresis lineage so rehabilitation-driven selector behavior cannot drift by runtime-local controllers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling governance).
Iteration 33 formalizes deterministic multi-family coupling-profile governance with bounded portfolio selection, deterministic family upgrades, and non-regressive fallback lineage so coupling-family choice cannot drift by runtime-local profile registries (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile governance).
Iteration 34 formalizes deterministic coupling-profile evidence-integrity governance with canonical upgrade/fallback signal attestation, stale-signal tolerance bounds, and anti-regression proof lineage so coupling-family admissibility cannot drift by runtime-local telemetry/proof adapters (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity governance).
Iteration 35 formalizes deterministic coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration governance with multi-attester evidence weighting, proof-expiry cutovers, and dispute-class adaptive tolerance-band lineage so evidence-driven coupling admissibility cannot drift by runtime-local trust/TTL adapters (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration governance).
Iteration 36 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration portfolio governance with bounded weighting-family portfolios, proof-expiry debt carryover lineage, and cross-dispute fairness caps so trust-calibration profile choice and debt/fairness transitions cannot drift by runtime-local schedulers or debt ledgers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio governance).
Iteration 37 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio stability governance with canonical debt-amortization ladders, fairness-cap rebound controls, and policy hysteresis freezes so debt/fairness rebalancing cannot drift by runtime-local amortizers or rebound controllers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability governance).
Iteration 38 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability family governance with bounded amortization-ladder portfolio selection, deterministic freeze liftoff cutovers, and non-regressive rebound fallback lineage so family selection/liftoff/fallback cannot drift by runtime-local ladder registries or rebound controllers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family governance).
Iteration 39 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family evidence handoff governance with canonical freeze-liftoff debt handoff attestations, rebound-fallback forgiveness-ledger monotonicity, and bounded family-proof carryforward lineage so handoff outcomes cannot drift by runtime-local debt verifiers or forgiveness/proof caches (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff governance).
Iteration 40 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff portfolio governance with bounded forgiveness-ledger replenishment, carryforward expiry-cliff control, and debt-handoff attester quorum-degradation lineage so handoff continuity outcomes cannot drift by runtime-local replenishment schedulers, proof-expiry cliff evaluators, or quorum-health caches (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio governance).
Iteration 41 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio resilience governance with bounded replenishment debt carryforward, expiry-cliff smoothing cutovers, and quorum-recovery probation lineage so resilience-state restoration cannot drift by runtime-local debt carryforward calculators, cliff smoothers, or probation timers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience governance).
Iteration 42 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience family governance with bounded debt-carryforward ladder portfolios, smoothing-cutover hysteresis classes, and probation-exit restitution lineage so resilience-family state cannot drift by runtime-local ladder selectors, hysteresis controllers, or restitution calculators (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience family governance).
Iteration 43 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family settlement governance with restitution-clawback-window closure, ladder-demotion quarantine progression, and hysteresis debt-cooling cutover lineage so settlement closure cannot drift by runtime-local clawback/quarantine/cooling controllers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience family settlement governance).
Iteration 44 formalizes deterministic trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement finality governance with clawback-appeal escrow closure, ladder-demotion quarantine-release quorum, and debt-cooling reentry-cutover lineage so finality closure cannot drift by runtime-local appeal/quorum/reentry controllers (Design Target 4 + 5 + 6 closure on disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling-profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience family settlement-finality governance).

## Core Entities and Identifiers (v0.60)

1. `EntityNode`
- `entity_id`: stable ID from entity canonicalization layer.
- `aliases`: lexical forms that may map to the same `entity_id`.

2. `ClaimType`
- `claim_type_id`: ontology predicate ID with version (example: `org.ceo_of@v3`).
- `roles`: ordered role schema with type constraints (example: `subject:Person`, `object:Organization`).

3. `ClaimCore`
- Semantic identity of a proposition independent of provenance and revision events.
- `core_id = H(ns, claim_type_id, role_fingerprint, polarity, quantifier, modality)`.
- `role_fingerprint`: lexicographically sorted `role=value_token` pairs.

4. `ClaimRevision`
- Immutable assertion payload bound to one `core_id` and one normalized valid-time interval.
- `revision_id = H(ns_rev, core_id, valid_interval_fingerprint, assertion_kind, payload_fingerprint)`.
- `assertion_kind`: `observed` | `inferred`.
- `origin_tx_id` optional:
set only when revision was projected from a quarantined foreign epoch op;
preserves source chronology for precedence ranking while visibility is still gated by local assertion tx.
- `justification_policy`:
`none` | `requires_support(min_support, min_conf_bp)` | `requires_derivation(min_witnesses, min_conf_bp)`.

5. `RevisionStatusEvent`
- Append-only lifecycle event for one `revision_id`.
- `status_event_id = H(ns_rev_evt, revision_id, event_type, cause_fingerprint, tx_id)`.
- `event_type`: `asserted` | `superseded` | `retracted` | `auto_retracted`.

6. `EvidenceAtom`
- `evidence_id`: stable ID for an atomic evidence payload (document span, measurement, sensor event, etc.).
- Contains source metadata and extraction fingerprint; does not encode claim truth directly.
- Deterministic quality fields:
`source_reliability_bp` (`0..10000`), `extraction_quality_bp` (`0..10000`), `independence_key`.

7. `RelationRecord`
- Canonical, hash-addressed relation instance.
- `relation_id = H(ns_rel, relation_type_id, endpoint_fingerprint, qualifier_fingerprint)`.
- Relation semantics are typed by `relation_type_id`; endpoint normalization is relation-specific.

8. `RuleRecord`
- `rule_id`: stable identifier of deterministic inference rule implementation + version.
- `rule_reliability_bp`: calibrated reliability prior in basis points (`0..10000`).
- Rule implementation is immutable per version; upgrades require new `rule_id`.

9. `TransactionRecord`
- `tx_id`: globally comparable tuple `(hlc_ms, replica_id, replica_seq)`.
- `status`: `committed` | `voided`.
- `voided_at_tx_id` optional; supports rollback as a first-class transaction-time event.

10. `SchemaEpoch`
- `schema_epoch_id`: immutable ID for the full canonicalization + invariant bundle used to compute IDs and admission decisions.
- Any change to normalization/invariant policy requires a new epoch ID.

11. `OperationEnvelope`
- Replication unit for append-only sync.
- `op_id = H(ns_op, schema_epoch_id, tx_id, op_kind, subject_id, payload_hash)`.
- `op_kind`: `upsert_core` | `assert_revision` | `append_status_event` | `assert_relation` | `upsert_evidence` | `upsert_rule` | `void_tx` | `upsert_interval_strategy_policy` | `apply_interval_surgery` | `upsert_epoch_path_policy` | `apply_epoch_migration` | `apply_migration_compaction` | `upsert_base_payload_capsule` | `upsert_retention_policy` | `apply_retention_gc` | `upsert_capsule_cache_policy` | `apply_capsule_cache_lease` | `upsert_capsule_profile_policy` | `apply_capsule_profile_rotation` | `upsert_capsule_tier_policy_family` | `upsert_capsule_tier_telemetry_policy` | `apply_capsule_tier_telemetry` | `apply_capsule_tier_utility` | `apply_capsule_tier_assignment` | `upsert_capsule_global_budget_policy` | `upsert_capsule_attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_evidence_integrity_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_policy` | `apply_capsule_attester_coupling_portfolio_debt_amortization` | `apply_capsule_attester_coupling_fairness_rebalance` | `apply_capsule_attester_coupling_portfolio_hysteresis_freeze`.

12. `ConflictRecord`
- Deterministic record for merge/admission conflicts that are not silently auto-resolved.
- `conflict_id = H(ns_conflict, conflict_class, subject_fingerprint, first_seen_tx_id)`.
- Carries `conflict_class`, involved IDs, resolution state (`open` | `resolved`), and optional chosen winner.

13. `IntervalSurgeryRecord`
- Immutable projection record for resolving one overlap component for one `core_id`.
- `surgery_id = H(ns_surg, core_id, component_id, basis_hash, segment_plan_fingerprint, tx_id)`.
- Contains `component_id`, `basis_hash`, `strategy_id`, `policy_seq`, `strategy_policy_hash`, `strategy_basis_hash`, `surgery_projection_key`, `plan_tx_asof`, and canonical `segment_plan`.

14. `IntervalStrategyPolicy`
- Immutable strategy-governance policy for one `claim_type_id`.
- `interval_policy_id = H(ns_is_policy, claim_type_id, policy_seq, strategy_id, admissibility_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `policy_seq` is monotonic per `claim_type_id` and defines deterministic cutover precedence.
- Current strategy families:
`tx_recency_rev_tiebreak_v1` (rank key `(precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)`),
`confidence_then_precedence_v1` (rank key `(confidence_bp_plan DESC, precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)`).
- Current admissibility profile:
`deterministic_interval_rank_inputs_v1` (all rank inputs must be canonical serializations over deterministic revision/provenance functions).

15. `EpochMigrationSpec`
- Immutable, versioned projection spec for one migration path.
- `migration_spec_id = H(ns_mspec, source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, projector_version, projector_hash)`.
- Defines deterministic pure function:
`project_op(source_op_bytes) -> projected_candidate | non_projectable(reason_code)`.

16. `EpochMigrationRecord`
- Immutable record for one applied migration batch.
- `migration_id = H(ns_migr, source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, migration_spec_id, source_set_hash, projection_hash, tx_id)`.
- Contains sorted `source_op_ids`, `source_set_hash`, `projection_hash`, and links to produced projected op keys.

17. `EpochPathPolicy`
- Immutable path-governance policy for one `target_epoch_id`.
- `path_policy_id = H(ns_mpath_policy, target_epoch_id, policy_seq, selector_id, selector_hash)`.
- `policy_seq` is monotonic per `target_epoch_id` and defines deterministic cutover precedence.
- Current selector family: `shortest_path_spec_lex_v1` with deterministic rank key `(hop_count ASC, path_fingerprint ASC)`.

18. `MigrationCompactionRecord`
- Immutable metadata compaction record over already-admitted migration outputs.
- `compaction_id = H(ns_mcmp, target_epoch_id, policy_seq, basis_hash, dominated_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- Contains deterministic `winner_set` and `dominated_set` of migration projection versions; it never mutates base revision payloads.

19. `RetentionPolicy`
- Immutable policy-governance record for one reclaimable artifact class.
- `retention_policy_id = H(ns_rpol, artifact_class, policy_seq, proof_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `artifact_class`: `migration_projected_version` | `interval_surgery_projection` | `claim_revision_payload` | `evidence_atom_payload` | `rule_record_payload`.
- `policy_seq` is monotonic per `artifact_class` and defines deterministic retention cutover precedence.
- Current proof profile families:
`rehydratable_from_retained_lineage_v1` (derived projections),
`rehydratable_from_base_capsule_v1` (base payload classes).

20. `RetentionGcRecord`
- Immutable physical-reclamation record for one deterministic GC batch.
- `retention_gc_id = H(ns_rgc, artifact_class, policy_seq, gc_basis_hash, reclaimed_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- Contains sorted `reclaimed_artifact_keys`, per-key `artifact_payload_hash`, immutable `rehydration_manifest_hash`, and optional `semantic_kernel_hash`; it never mutates logical query semantics.

21. `BasePayloadCapsuleRecord`
- Immutable content-addressed capsule for reclaimable canonical base payload bytes.
- `capsule_id = H(ns_bcap, artifact_class, artifact_key, artifact_payload_hash, codec_profile_id, key_epoch_seq, capsule_manifest_hash)`.
- Stores deterministic cold-tier payload bytes/chunks referenced by retention manifests; semantic-kernel fields remain outside capsule and never GC-reclaimable.

22. `CapsuleCachePolicy`
- Immutable cache-governance policy for one base `artifact_class`.
- `capsule_cache_policy_id = H(ns_ccpol, artifact_class, policy_seq, cache_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `artifact_class` is constrained to base payload classes:
`claim_revision_payload` | `evidence_atom_payload` | `rule_record_payload`.
- `policy_seq` is monotonic per `artifact_class` and defines deterministic cache-policy cutover precedence.
- Current cache profile family:
`bounded_rehydrate_latency_v1` (requires active warm lease for every visible reclaimed key, deterministic eviction gating, and explicit max-latency contract fields).

23. `CapsuleCacheLeaseRecord`
- Immutable cache-residency transition record for one `capsule_id`.
- `cache_lease_id = H(ns_clease, capsule_id, artifact_class, policy_seq, lease_seq, lease_kind, cache_basis_hash, tx_id)`.
- `lease_kind`: `warm` | `evict`.
- Contains:
`cache_policy_hash`, `cache_basis_hash`, `lease_seq`, `lease_expiry_tx_id` (for warm leases), `cache_image_hash`, and sorted `pinned_gc_artifact_keys`.
- Defines deterministic warm-tier residency state without mutating logical claim/relation semantics.

24. `CapsuleProfilePolicy`
- Immutable codec/key-governance policy for one base `artifact_class`.
- `capsule_profile_policy_id = H(ns_cppol, artifact_class, policy_seq, write_codec_profile_id, write_key_epoch_seq, read_compat_set_hash, rotation_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `policy_seq` is monotonic per `artifact_class` and defines deterministic codec/key cutover precedence.
- `read_compat_set_hash = H(ns_cpcompat, sorted(tuple(codec_profile_id, key_epoch_seq)))`.
- Current rotation profile family:
`deterministic_transcode_rewrap_v1` (decode canonical payload bytes, then deterministically encode/rewrap under target codec/key tuple).

25. `CapsuleProfileRotationRecord`
- Immutable record for deterministic capsule rebinding of reclaimed base artifact keys.
- `capsule_rotation_id = H(ns_cprot, artifact_class, policy_seq, profile_policy_hash, rotation_basis_hash, rotated_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(gc_artifact_key, source_capsule_id, target_capsule_id, rotation_projection_key, artifact_payload_hash)`.
- `rotation_projection_key = H(ns_cprotk, artifact_class, gc_artifact_key, policy_seq, target_codec_profile_id, target_key_epoch_seq)`.

26. `CapsuleTierPolicyFamily`
- Immutable workload-tier policy-family record for one base `artifact_class`.
- `capsule_tier_policy_family_id = H(ns_ctpf, artifact_class, family_seq, selector_profile_id, admissibility_profile_id, cost_profile_id, tier_defs_hash, policy_hash)`.
- `family_seq` is monotonic per `artifact_class` and defines deterministic tier-family cutover precedence.
- `tier_defs_hash = H(ns_ctierdefs, sorted(tuple(tier_id, tier_rank, cache_policy_hash, profile_policy_hash, max_warm_bytes, max_rotate_bytes, max_rehydrate_ops, overflow_rank)))`.
- Each tier references one cache/profile member policy hash and deterministic per-tier bounded-cost limits.

27. `CapsuleTierAssignmentRecord`
- Immutable assignment record that binds reclaimed base keys to workload tiers under one active family.
- `capsule_tier_assignment_id = H(ns_ctas, artifact_class, family_seq, assignment_seq, tier_family_policy_hash, tier_basis_hash, assigned_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `assignment_seq` is monotonic per `(artifact_class, family_seq)` and defines deterministic assignment precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(gc_artifact_key, capsule_id, tier_id, tier_projection_key, selector_feature_hash, utility_bp, predicted_warm_bytes, predicted_rotate_bytes, predicted_rehydrate_ops)`.
- `tier_projection_key = H(ns_ctpk, artifact_class, gc_artifact_key, family_seq, assignment_seq)`.

28. `CapsuleTierTelemetryPolicy`
- Immutable telemetry-governance policy for one base `artifact_class` and one tier family generation.
- `capsule_tier_telemetry_policy_id = H(ns_cttp, artifact_class, family_seq, telemetry_seq, window_profile_id, utility_profile_id, anti_gaming_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `telemetry_seq` is monotonic per `(artifact_class, family_seq)` and defines deterministic telemetry cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic lookback windows, observer contribution caps, and anti-gaming guard thresholds.

29. `CapsuleTierTelemetryRecord`
- Immutable telemetry aggregate record for one reclaimed key, one telemetry window, and one observer bucket.
- `capsule_tier_telemetry_id = H(ns_cttel, artifact_class, family_seq, telemetry_seq, telemetry_window_id, gc_artifact_key, observer_bucket_id, telemetry_basis_hash, tx_id)`.
- Contains deterministic counters:
`served_reads`, `cache_hits`, `rehydrate_ops`, `rotate_ops`, `latency_saved_us_sum`, `distinct_request_hashes`.
- `telemetry_basis_hash = H(ns_cttbasis, artifact_class, family_seq, telemetry_seq, telemetry_window_id, gc_artifact_key, observer_bucket_id, sorted(request_hashes), counter_tuple_hash)`.

30. `CapsuleTierUtilityRecord`
- Immutable utility projection record derived from admitted telemetry aggregates.
- `capsule_tier_utility_id = H(ns_ctutil, artifact_class, family_seq, telemetry_seq, utility_seq, tier_telemetry_policy_hash, utility_basis_hash, projected_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `utility_seq` is monotonic per `(artifact_class, family_seq, telemetry_seq)` and defines deterministic utility precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(gc_artifact_key, utility_bp, anti_gaming_penalty_bp, distinct_observer_count, dominant_observer_share_bp, utility_projection_key)`.
- `utility_projection_key = H(ns_ctupk, artifact_class, gc_artifact_key, family_seq, telemetry_seq, utility_seq)`.

31. `CapsuleGlobalBudgetPolicy`
- Immutable global budget-governance policy spanning base payload artifact classes.
- `capsule_global_budget_policy_id = H(ns_cgbpol, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, fair_share_profile_id, sla_profile_id, global_caps_hash, class_weight_hash, policy_hash)`.
- `budget_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic global budget cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic global resource caps and class-level fair-share + SLA floor tuples over:
`max_global_warm_bytes`, `max_global_rotate_bytes`, `max_global_rehydrate_ops`.

32. `CapsuleGlobalBudgetArbitrationRecord`
- Immutable cross-artifact-class allocation record derived from active utility and demand snapshots.
- `capsule_global_budget_arbitration_id = H(ns_cgbarb, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, arbitration_seq, global_budget_policy_hash, global_budget_basis_hash, allocation_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `arbitration_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, budget_seq)` and defines deterministic arbitration precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(artifact_class, family_seq, allocated_warm_bytes, allocated_rotate_bytes, allocated_rehydrate_ops, demand_warm_bytes, demand_rotate_bytes, demand_rehydrate_ops, utility_mass_bp, fairness_debt_bp, sla_shortfall_bp, global_budget_projection_key)`.
- `global_budget_projection_key = H(ns_cgbpk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, arbitration_seq)`.

33. `CapsuleGlobalBudgetMemoryPolicy`
- Immutable long-horizon memory-governance policy for one `budget_domain_id`.
- `capsule_global_budget_memory_policy_id = H(ns_cgbmpol, budget_domain_id, memory_policy_seq, horizon_profile_id, burst_decay_profile_id, sla_debt_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `memory_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic memory-policy cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic carryover/decay caps:
`deficit_cap_bp`, `burst_credit_cap_bp`, `sla_debt_cap_bp`, plus decay ratios and repayment constraints.

34. `CapsuleGlobalBudgetMemoryRecord`
- Immutable per-window long-horizon memory state derived from prior memory + visible arbitration/utilization snapshots.
- `capsule_global_budget_memory_id = H(ns_cgbmem, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, memory_policy_seq, memory_seq, global_budget_memory_policy_hash, global_budget_memory_basis_hash, memory_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `memory_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, budget_seq, memory_policy_seq)` and defines deterministic memory precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(artifact_class, deficit_carry_bp, burst_credit_bp, sla_debt_bp, memory_projection_key)`.
- `memory_projection_key = H(ns_cgbmpk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, memory_policy_seq, memory_seq)`.

35. `CapsuleUtilizationAttestationPolicy`
- Immutable utilization-attestation governance policy for one `budget_domain_id`.
- `capsule_utilization_attestation_policy_id = H(ns_cguapol, budget_domain_id, utilization_policy_seq, window_profile_id, attester_quorum_profile_id, reconciliation_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `utilization_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic attestation-policy cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic closure + reconciliation bounds:
`close_lag_windows`, `reconcile_grace_windows`, `max_reconcile_delta_bp`, and canonical attester catalog constraints.

36. `CapsuleUtilizationAttestationRecord`
- Immutable utilization snapshot for one closed canonical window under one policy generation.
- `capsule_utilization_attestation_id = H(ns_cguatt, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, attestation_seq, utilization_attestation_policy_hash, utilization_basis_hash, attested_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `attestation_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id)` and defines deterministic attestation precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(artifact_class, used_warm_bytes, used_rotate_bytes, used_rehydrate_ops, attester_set_hash, attestation_projection_key)`.
- `attestation_projection_key = H(ns_cguapk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, attestation_seq)`.

37. `CapsuleUtilizationReconciliationRecord`
- Immutable late-window correction record for one closed utilization window.
- `capsule_utilization_reconciliation_id = H(ns_cgurec, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, reconciliation_seq, utilization_attestation_policy_hash, reconciliation_basis_hash, reconciliation_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `reconciliation_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id)` and defines deterministic reconciliation precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(artifact_class, delta_used_warm_bytes, delta_used_rotate_bytes, delta_used_rehydrate_ops, carry_forward_window_id, reconciliation_projection_key)`.
- `reconciliation_projection_key = H(ns_cgurecpk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, reconciliation_seq)`.

38. `CapsuleAttesterTrustPolicy`
- Immutable attester-trust governance policy for one `budget_domain_id`.
- `capsule_attester_trust_policy_id = H(ns_cgatpol, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, identity_profile_id, trust_tier_profile_id, cutover_profile_id, continuity_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `attester_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic attester-governance cutover precedence.
- Defines canonical attester roster constraints, trust-tier weighting bounds, dual-quorum cutover bounds, and historical continuity proof requirements.

39. `CapsuleAttesterSetRotationRecord`
- Immutable attester-set rotation record for one canonical utilization window under one attester-trust policy generation.
- `capsule_attester_set_rotation_id = H(ns_cgatrot, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, rotation_seq, attester_trust_policy_hash, attester_set_basis_hash, attester_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `rotation_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, utilization_window_id)` and defines deterministic rotation precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(attester_id, trust_tier_id, quorum_weight_bp, key_epoch_seq, set_role, rotation_projection_key)`.
- `rotation_projection_key = H(ns_cgatrotpk, budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, attester_id, attester_policy_seq, rotation_seq)`.

40. `CapsuleAttesterContinuityRecord`
- Immutable continuity certificate record bridging retired/rotated attester identities or keys for historical proof verification.
- `capsule_attester_continuity_id = H(ns_cgatcont, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_epoch_id, continuity_seq, attester_trust_policy_hash, attester_continuity_basis_hash, continuity_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `continuity_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_epoch_id)` and defines deterministic continuity precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, legacy_key_epoch_seq, successor_key_epoch_seq, continuity_proof_hash, continuity_projection_key)`.
- `continuity_projection_key = H(ns_cgatcontpk, budget_domain_id, continuity_epoch_id, legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_seq)`.

41. `CapsuleAttesterAccountabilityPolicy`
- Immutable attester-accountability governance policy for one `budget_domain_id`.
- `capsule_attester_accountability_policy_id = H(ns_cgaapol, budget_domain_id, accountability_policy_seq, fault_profile_id, slash_profile_id, decay_profile_id, reinstatement_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `accountability_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic accountability-policy cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic fault-class penalties, slash/debt caps, decay recovery bounds, probation windows, and reinstatement quorum gates.

42. `CapsuleAttesterAccountabilityRecord`
- Immutable per-attester accountability memory transition for one canonical utilization window.
- `capsule_attester_accountability_id = H(ns_cgaacc, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, accountability_seq, attester_accountability_policy_hash, accountability_basis_hash, accountability_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `accountability_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq, utilization_window_id)` and defines deterministic slashing/decay precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(attester_id, fault_class_id, slash_bp, slash_debt_bp, reputation_bp, probation_until_window_id, accountability_projection_key)`.
- `accountability_projection_key = H(ns_cgaaccpk, budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq, accountability_seq)`.

43. `CapsuleAttesterReinstatementRecord`
- Immutable reinstatement transition record for one attester under one accountability policy generation.
- `capsule_attester_reinstatement_id = H(ns_cgarein, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, attester_id, reinstatement_seq, attester_accountability_policy_hash, reinstatement_basis_hash, reinstatement_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `reinstatement_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq)` and defines deterministic reinstatement precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(attester_id, restored_trust_tier_id, restored_quorum_weight_bp, effective_from_window_id, reinstatement_projection_key)`.
- `reinstatement_projection_key = H(ns_cgareinpk, budget_domain_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq, reinstatement_seq)`.

44. `CapsuleAttesterAdjudicationPolicy`

- `capsule_attester_adjudication_policy_id = H(ns_cgajpol, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, jurisdiction_profile_id, verdict_profile_id, appeal_profile_id, finality_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
- `adjudication_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic adjudication-policy cutover precedence.
- Defines deterministic jurisdiction quorum requirements, verdict admissibility constraints, appeal-round bounds, and finality delays.

45. `CapsuleAttesterFaultAdjudicationRecord`
- Immutable cross-domain verdict record for one disputed fault lineage.
- `capsule_attester_fault_adjudication_id = H(ns_cgajadj, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, dispute_id, adjudication_seq, attester_adjudication_policy_hash, adjudication_basis_hash, adjudication_set_hash, tx_id)`.
- `adjudication_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, dispute_id, adjudication_policy_seq)` and defines deterministic verdict precedence.

`(dispute_id, attester_id, fault_event_id, jurisdiction_id, verdict_code, penalty_class_id, evidence_root_hash, adjudication_projection_key)`.

46. `CapsuleAttesterAppealFinalityRecord`

- `appeal_seq` is monotonic per `(budget_domain_id, dispute_id, adjudication_policy_seq)` and defines deterministic appeal/finality precedence.
- Contains sorted tuples:
`(dispute_id, attester_id, appeal_round, appeal_outcome_code, finality_state, finality_window_id, appeal_projection_key)`.

- `portability_policy_seq` is monotonic per `budget_domain_id` and defines deterministic portability-policy cutover precedence.

- `capsule_attester_portability_disclosure_policy_id = H(ns_cgajdpol, budget_domain_id, disclosure_policy_seq, redaction_commitment_profile_id, selective_reveal_profile_id, review_attestation_profile_id, privacy_budget_profile_id, policy_hash)`.

51. `CapsuleAttesterPortabilityDisclosureRecord`
- Immutable redaction commitment + selective-reveal record for one portability dispute lineage.
- `capsule_attester_portability_disclosure_id = H(ns_cgajdisc, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, portability_policy_seq, disclosure_policy_seq, dispute_id, disclosure_seq, attester_portability_disclosure_policy_hash, disclosure_basis_hash, disclosure_set_hash, tx_id)`.

`(dispute_id, attester_id, debt_epoch_id, quorum_tier_id, required_attester_count, observed_attester_count, degradation_stage_id, degradation_window_id, degradation_reason_code, coupling_debt_handoff_quorum_degradation_projection_key)`.
- `coupling_debt_handoff_quorum_degradation_projection_key = H(ns_cgajlsigfedrehabobjprofevitrustportstabfamhandoffportquorumpk, budget_domain_id, dispute_id, attester_id, disclosure_policy_seq, lifecycle_policy_seq, lifecycle_calibration_policy_seq, objective_policy_seq, signal_integrity_policy_seq, federation_policy_seq, rehabilitation_policy_seq, coupling_policy_seq, coupling_profile_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_portfolio_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_handoff_policy_seq, coupling_profile_evidence_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_handoff_portfolio_policy_seq, coupling_debt_handoff_quorum_degradation_seq)`.

## Semantic Identity Mechanism

### Input Contract

`DSFG` identity does not ingest raw text directly. Ingestion requires a structured candidate:
- `claim_type_id`
- `role_bindings`
- `polarity` (`affirmed` | `negated`)
- `quantifier` (`exists`, `all`, `most`, etc.)
- `modality` (`asserted`, `alleged`, `counterfactual`, etc.)

Any model-based extraction happens before this interface and is treated as untrusted input.

### Canonicalization Pipeline (Deterministic)

1. Validate schema:
- Reject if any role violates `ClaimType` constraints.

2. Normalize each role value:
- Entity role: map lexical aliases to `entity_id`.
- Scalar role: normalize unit + precision policy (example: `10 km` -> `10000 m`).
- Temporal literal role: normalize to canonical interval syntax.
- Unknown role: encode explicit sentinel `UNK::<role_name>` (never wildcard).

3. Build `role_fingerprint`:
- Sort `role=value_token` pairs by role name.

4. Compute `core_id` hash:
- Hash input order is fixed and versioned by namespace `ns`.

### Identity Invariants

- `INV-S1`: Two claims share `core_id` if and only if all canonical identity fields match exactly.
- `INV-S2`: Role ordering in input cannot change `core_id`.
- `INV-S3`: Missing information does not merge with known information (`UNK::role` is identity-significant).
- `INV-S4`: Opposite polarity must yield different `core_id`s even with identical roles.
- `INV-S5`: Ontology version changes cannot silently rewrite identity (`claim_type_id` is versioned).

## Relation Algebra (v0.2)

### Relation Type Signatures

1. `contradicts`
- Kind: binary, symmetric.
- Endpoint types: `ClaimCore`, `ClaimCore`.
- Canonical endpoint order: sort ascending by `core_id`.
- Additional precondition: endpoints must be polarity complements over identical
`(claim_type_id, role_fingerprint, quantifier, modality)`.

2. `depends_on`
- Kind: binary, directed.
- Endpoint types: `ClaimCore` (dependent), `ClaimCore` (prerequisite).
- Canonical endpoint order: fixed as `(dependent_core_id, prerequisite_core_id)`.
- Interpretation: dependent claim requires prerequisite claim in the active reasoning policy.

3. `supports`
- Kind: binary, directed.
- Endpoint types: `ClaimRevision` (claim assertion), `EvidenceAtom`.
- Canonical endpoint order: fixed as `(revision_id, evidence_id)`.
- Interpretation: evidence contributes support to that specific revision event.

4. `derived_from`
- Kind: n-ary, directed hyperrelation.
- Endpoint types: one `ClaimRevision` conclusion + set of premise `ClaimRevision`s.
- Required qualifiers:
`rule_id`, `basis_mode`, `witness_basis_keys`, `basis_hash`.
- `basis_mode` current value: `lineage_anchor_union_v1`.
- `witness_basis_keys`:
sorted unique lineage anchor keys for this witness; if no anchor keys are derivable at assertion boundary, use sentinel
`RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT`.
- `basis_hash = H(ns_wbasis, rule_id, basis_mode, sorted_unique(witness_basis_keys))`.
- Canonical endpoint order:
`(conclusion_revision_id, sorted_unique(premise_revision_ids), rule_id, basis_mode, sorted_unique(witness_basis_keys), basis_hash)`.
- Interpretation: conclusion revision was inferred by deterministic rule application.

### Relation Invariants

- `INV-R1`: Every relation type enforces endpoint type discipline before ID computation.
- `INV-R2`: Symmetric relation endpoints are canonically sorted; insertion order cannot create duplicate IDs.
- `INV-R3`: Directed relation orientation is identity-significant.
- `INV-R4`: `contradicts` links are valid only for strict polarity complements under same non-polar identity fields.
- `INV-R5`: `derived_from` identity is invariant to premise list permutation and duplicate premises.
- `INV-R6`: `derived_from` and `supports` operate on `ClaimRevision`, not `ClaimCore`.
- `INV-R7`: No relation may create immediate self-loop:
`depends_on(core_x, core_x)` and `derived_from(rev_x, premises including rev_x)` are rejected.
- `INV-R8`: Relation activity is transaction-scoped; queries include only relation assertions whose owning transactions are visible at `tx_asof`.

### Deterministic Relation Operations

1. `assert_relation(candidate_relation) -> relation_id | rejection`
- Validate relation type and endpoint types.
- Normalize endpoints per signature.
- Validate relation-specific preconditions.
- Provenance boundary checks:
for `supports`, target revision must have `assertion_kind=observed`;
for `derived_from`, conclusion revision must have `assertion_kind=inferred`.
- Compute `relation_id` from canonical bytes.
- Upsert immutable canonical payload at `relation_id`.
- Record assertion event keyed by `tx_id`.

2. `auto_link_contradiction(core_id)`
- Compute complement key by flipping polarity and preserving all other identity fields.
- If complement `core_id` exists, assert `contradicts` using symmetric canonical ordering.

3. `assert_derivation(conclusion_revision_id, premise_revision_ids, rule_id, tx_id)`
- Deduplicate + sort premise revisions.
- Reject empty premise set.
- Reject if conclusion revision appears in premises.
- Compute canonical witness basis:
`compute_witness_basis_keys(premise_revision_ids, rule_id, tx_id)`.
- Compute `basis_hash`.
- Assert `derived_from` relation using canonical hyperrelation key + basis qualifiers.

4. `get_neighbors(node_ref, relation_type_id, direction, tx_asof) -> ordered_list`
- Deterministic ordering: ascending by `relation_id`.
- Include only relations whose assertion transactions are visible at `tx_asof`.
- Direction handling:
`out`, `in`, `both` for directed relations; ignored for symmetric relations.

## Dual-Time Semantics (v0.3)

### Temporal Primitives

1. Valid time (`VT`)
- Every revision has one normalized valid interval:
`valid_interval = [valid_from, valid_to)` where `valid_to` may be `+INF`.

2. Transaction time (`TT`)
- Every assertion/lifecycle event belongs to one `tx_id`.
- `tx_id` ordering is lexicographic over `(hlc_ms, replica_id, replica_seq)`.

3. Transaction visibility
- `tx_visible(tx_x, tx_asof) = true` iff:
`commit_tx(tx_x) <= tx_asof` and (`void_tx(tx_x)` absent or `void_tx(tx_x) > tx_asof`).
- This makes rollback time-aware: future queries reflect voiding; historical queries before voiding remain reproducible.

4. Precedence time (ranking key)
- `precedence_tx_id(revision)` is used for deterministic winner ranking:
`origin_tx_id` when present, else assertion event `tx_id`.
- Visibility and precedence are intentionally decoupled:
visibility gates on local assertion/migration transaction visibility,
ranking uses preserved source chronology when available.

### Revision Lifecycle Semantics

`ClaimRevision` payload is immutable; lifecycle is derived from visible status events.

1. Gather visible lifecycle events for revision `r` at `tx_asof`.
2. Sort by key `(tx_id ASC, event_rank ASC, status_event_id ASC)` with:
`event_rank(asserted)=1`, `event_rank(superseded)=2`, `event_rank(retracted)=2`, `event_rank(auto_retracted)=2`.
3. Fold finite-state machine:
- Start `inactive`.
- On `asserted`: `active`.
- On `superseded`/`retracted`/`auto_retracted`: `inactive`.
4. Result is `lifecycle_active(r, tx_asof)`.

### Temporal Invariants

- `INV-T1`: Every `ClaimRevision` has exactly one normalized half-open valid interval.
- `INV-T2`: `tx_id` comparison is total and deterministic across replicas.
- `INV-T3`: Revision payload fields (`core_id`, `valid_interval`, `assertion_kind`, payload) are immutable post-creation.
- `INV-T4`: Lifecycle state is a pure function of visible status events and `tx_asof`.
- `INV-T5`: Supersession is allowed only between revisions with identical `(core_id, valid_interval)` and strictly newer `tx_id`.
- `INV-T6`: Rollback is represented by transaction voiding, not by destructive deletion.
- `INV-T7`: `inferred` revisions are visible only if at least `min_witnesses` active `derived_from` witnesses hold at `tx_asof` and confidence meets policy threshold.
- `INV-T8`: Support-required revisions are visible only if active support classes meet `min_support` at `tx_asof` and confidence meets policy threshold.
- `INV-T9`: `as_of` lookup is two-dimensional and deterministic: filter by `VT` and `TT` before any ranking.
- `INV-T10`: `precedence_tx_id` is a deterministic pure function of immutable revision payload (`origin_tx_id`) and assertion lifecycle events; it affects ranking only, never visibility gating.

### Deterministic Temporal Operations

1. `assert_revision(candidate_revision, tx_id) -> revision_id | rejection`
- Normalize `valid_interval` and verify `valid_from < valid_to` unless open-ended.
- Compute deterministic `revision_id`.
- Upsert immutable `ClaimRevision` payload.
- Append `asserted` `RevisionStatusEvent` at `tx_id`.
- Deterministic supersession step:
close any currently active revision with identical `(core_id, valid_interval)` by appending `superseded` events, processed in ascending `revision_id`.

2. `void_transaction(target_tx_id, tx_now)`
- Mark `target_tx_id` as `voided` at `tx_now` (append-only transaction ledger update).
- Build impacted set:
revisions directly touched by events in `target_tx_id`, then transitive dependents through active `derived_from` and support-required links.
- Recompute justification in deterministic fixpoint order (ascending `revision_id`).
- Append `auto_retracted` events for revisions that are still lifecycle-active but no longer justified.

3. `revision_justified(revision_id, tx_asof) -> bool`
- Compute `confidence_bp = confidence_as_of(revision_id, tx_asof)`.
- If policy `none`: require `confidence_bp >= 0`.
- If `requires_support(k, min_conf_bp)`: require active support class count `>= k` and `confidence_bp >= min_conf_bp`.
- If `assertion_kind=inferred` or policy `requires_derivation(k, min_conf_bp)`:
require at least `k` active derivation witnesses where each premise revision is lifecycle-active at `tx_asof`, and require `confidence_bp >= min_conf_bp`.

4. `query_as_of(core_id, valid_at, tx_asof, mode) -> ordered revisions | single | ambiguous`
- Candidate filter:
`revision.core_id == core_id`
AND `valid_at in revision.valid_interval`
AND `lifecycle_active(revision, tx_asof)`
AND `revision_justified(revision, tx_asof)`.
- Exact-slot arbitration:
for each exact `valid_interval` bucket, keep only `resolve_exact_slot(core_id, valid_interval, tx_asof)` winner if present.
- Interval-component arbitration:
if remaining candidates at `valid_at` still form a partial-overlap component, call
`resolve_interval_component(core_id, valid_at, tx_asof)` and apply outcome:
`winner_revision_id` => keep only winner;
`null` => return empty for that `valid_at`;
`ambiguous` => only strict mode may return `AMBIGUOUS`.
- Order key (deterministic):
`precedence_tx_id DESC`, then `revision_id ASC`.
- `mode=all`: return ordered list.
- `mode=single_latest`: return first or null.
- `mode=single_strict`: return first only if list size is 1 and interval-component resolver did not return `ambiguous`; else `AMBIGUOUS`.

5. `query_lineage_as_of(revision_id, tx_asof)`
- Return deterministic lineage closure through visible `derived_from` and `supports` edges, ordered by `(depth ASC, relation_id ASC)`.

## Provenance and Uncertainty Semantics (v0.4 + v0.8 + v0.12 bridge-safe witness aggregation)

### Confidence Domain

Confidence is encoded as integer basis points (`bp`) to avoid floating-point nondeterminism:

- `BP = {0..10000}`.
- `mul_bp(a, b) = floor((a * b) / 10000)`.
- `or_bp(a, b) = 10000 - floor(((10000 - a) * (10000 - b)) / 10000)`.
- Multi-input fold order is deterministic: ascending key order of the grouped contributors.

### Provenance Primitives

1. Support contribution weight:
- For visible support edge `(revision_id -> evidence_id)`:
`support_bp = mul_bp(source_reliability_bp(evidence_id), extraction_quality_bp(evidence_id))`.

2. Evidence dependence control:
- Support edges are grouped by `independence_key`.
- Group contribution is `max(support_bp)` within the group (conservative de-duplication).
- Final observed aggregation is noisy-or over groups in ascending `independence_key` order.

3. Derivation witness contribution:
- For one visible `derived_from` witness with qualifier `rule_id`:
`witness_bp = mul_bp(rule_reliability_bp(rule_id), min(premise_conf_bp_set))`.
- Premise confidence uses `confidence_as_of(premise_revision_id, tx_asof)`.

4. Witness basis derivation (assertion-time snapshot):
- For `assert_derivation(..., tx_assert)`, compute premise anchor closure:
if premise revision is observed, use visible support `independence_key`s at `tx_assert`;
if premise revision is inferred, use union of `witness_basis_keys` from visible incoming `derived_from` witnesses at `tx_assert`.
- Witness basis keys are the sorted unique union across premise closures.
- If this union is empty, substitute sentinel `RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT`.
- Persist immutable qualifiers on relation payload:
`basis_mode=lineage_anchor_union_v1`,
`witness_basis_keys`,
`basis_hash`.

5. Rule-level bridge-safe dependence control for inference:
- Group visible witnesses by `rule_id`.
- For one rule bucket:
if any witness has sentinel basis key `RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT`,
use conservative fallback `rule_bp = max(witness_bp)` for that rule bucket.
- Otherwise derive deterministic anchor-mass split per witness:
for witness basis key count `n`, assign integer `mass_bp` shares that sum to `10000`:
`base = floor(10000 / n)`, `rem = 10000 - base*n`,
and in ascending `anchor_key` order assign `mass_bp = base + 1` to first `rem` anchors, else `base`.
- For witness `w` and anchor `k`, allocated contribution:
`alloc_bp(w,k) = floor((witness_bp(w) * mass_bp(w,k)) / 10000)`.
- Anchor contribution:
`anchor_bp(k) = max(alloc_bp(w,k))` over witnesses containing anchor `k`.
- Rule bucket contribution:
`anchor_or_bp = noisy_or(anchor_bp(k))` in ascending `anchor_key` order,
`strongest_witness_bp = max(witness_bp(w))`,
`rule_bp = max(strongest_witness_bp, anchor_or_bp)`.
- Distinct `rule_id` buckets still combine via noisy-or in ascending `rule_id` order.

### Assertion-Kind Boundary

- `observed` revisions derive confidence only from `supports` evidence groups.
- `inferred` revisions derive confidence only from `derived_from` witnesses and rule reliability.
- Mixed-mode confidence is disallowed to keep lineage explainability and update behavior deterministic.
If a fact is both observed and inferred, represent them as separate revisions under the same `core_id`.

### Provenance and Confidence Invariants

- `INV-P1`: Confidence arithmetic is integer-only (`bp` domain) with fixed rounding (`floor`).
- `INV-P2`: `confidence_as_of(revision_id, tx_asof)` is a pure function of visible revisions, visible relations, visible transactions, evidence atoms, and rule catalog at `tx_asof`.
- `INV-P3`: Duplicate supports with the same `independence_key` cannot increase contribution above the group maximum.
- `INV-P4`: `observed` revisions cannot be the conclusion endpoint of active `derived_from` relations.
- `INV-P5`: `inferred` revisions cannot have active incoming `supports` edges.
- `INV-P6`: Inference confidence for a witness is bounded by the weakest premise confidence and rule reliability.
- `INV-P7`: Same-rule witness multiplicity cannot inflate confidence for one anchor key; only the strongest allocated anchor contribution contributes for that key.
- `INV-P8`: Confidence computation is monotone over the inference graph and converges to a least fixpoint in finite steps due to bounded discrete lattice.
- `INV-P9`: Lineage explanation output is canonicalized and replay-stable (sorted keys, stable tie-breakers).
- `INV-P10`: Justification policy gates (`min_support`, `min_witnesses`, `min_conf_bp`) are enforced after confidence computation in deterministic order.
- `INV-P11`: Every admitted `derived_from` witness has canonical `basis_mode`, `witness_basis_keys`, and `basis_hash`; empty basis is represented only by sentinel `RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT`.
- `INV-P12`: Witness `basis_hash` must match deterministic recomputation from premise lineage closure at witness assertion boundary; mismatch is conflict `CF-14`.
- `INV-P13`: For non-sentinel witnesses, anchor-mass split is deterministic from sorted `witness_basis_keys` and sums exactly to `10000 bp`.
- `INV-P14`: For one anchor key, duplicate/overlapping witnesses cannot inflate contribution above deterministic `max(alloc_bp)` for that anchor.
- `INV-P15`: Rule-local contribution is bridge-safe and deterministic:
`max(strongest_witness_bp, noisy_or(anchor_bp(k)))` with canonical `anchor_key` ordering.
- `INV-P16`: Presence of sentinel basis key in a rule bucket enforces conservative fallback `rule_bp = max(witness_bp)`; anchor accumulation is disabled for that bucket.
- `INV-P17`: Rule-level contribution for equal visible witness sets and premise confidences is arrival-order independent.
- `INV-P18`: `explain_confidence_as_of` for inferred revisions includes canonical per-witness anchor-mass allocation and per-anchor maxima.

### Deterministic Confidence Operations

1. `confidence_observed(revision_id, tx_asof) -> bp`
- Gather visible support edges for `revision_id`.
- Compute each `support_bp`.
- Group by `independence_key`; take per-group `max`.
- Fold with `or_bp` in ascending `independence_key`.

2. `compute_witness_basis_keys(premise_revision_ids, rule_id, tx_asof) -> sorted_keys, basis_hash`
- For each premise revision:
if observed, gather visible support `independence_key`s at `tx_asof`;
if inferred, gather visible incoming witness `witness_basis_keys` at `tx_asof`.
- Union, sort, unique.
- If empty, return sentinel set `{RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT}`.
- Return sorted keys plus
`basis_hash = H(ns_wbasis, rule_id, basis_mode=lineage_anchor_union_v1, sorted_keys)`.

3. `compute_witness_anchor_mass(witness_basis_keys) -> ordered_anchor_mass`
- If basis contains sentinel `RULE::<rule_id>::DEPENDENT`, return sentinel-only mass vector `{sentinel:10000}`.
- Else for `n = len(sorted_unique(witness_basis_keys))`:
`base = floor(10000 / n)`, `rem = 10000 - base*n`.
- Emit ordered tuples `(anchor_key, mass_bp)` in ascending `anchor_key` order with deterministic remainder distribution:
first `rem` anchors get `base+1`, remaining anchors get `base`.

4. `confidence_inferred_fixpoint(tx_asof) -> map<revision_id, bp>`
- Build active revision set at `tx_asof`.
- Initialize:
`conf[r] = confidence_observed(r, tx_asof)` for observed revisions,
`conf[r] = 0` for inferred revisions.
- Iterate revisions in ascending `revision_id` until no changes:
recompute inferred confidence from visible derivation witnesses using current premise confidences,
rule-bucket bridge-safe anchor aggregation, then cross-rule noisy-or.
- Return least fixpoint map.

5. `confidence_as_of(revision_id, tx_asof) -> bp`
- Read from `confidence_inferred_fixpoint(tx_asof)` by `revision_id`.

6. `explain_confidence_as_of(revision_id, tx_asof) -> certificate`
- Return canonical explanation payload:
`final_conf_bp`,
`support_groups` (for observed) or `witness_rule_anchors` (for inferred),
leaf evidence closure from `query_lineage_as_of`.
- Output ordering:
ascending by `independence_key`, `rule_id`, `anchor_key`, then `relation_id`.

## Distributed Sync and Convergence Semantics (v0.5, integrated with v0.6-v0.60)

### Merge Kernel

Replica state is modeled as immutable keyed sets plus deterministic derived views.
Merge of two replicas is key-union on each base set followed by deterministic reduction.

For any keyed set `K`:
- `merge(K_a, K_b) = K_a UNION K_b` by key.
- If same key appears with byte-identical payload: keep one.
- If same key appears with different payload: classify conflict `CF-03` and mark key as poisoned (excluded from admitted graph until resolved).

### Conflict Taxonomy and Resolution Outcomes

1. `CF-01 duplicate_op`
- Trigger: same `op_id`, same payload from multiple replicas.
- Resolution: idempotent no-op; one retained.

2. `CF-02 schema_epoch_mismatch`
- Trigger: incoming op has unsupported `schema_epoch_id`.
- Resolution: quarantine op; emit `ConflictRecord`; do not admit to canonical graph.

3. `CF-03 payload_mismatch_same_id`
- Trigger: same identity key, divergent payload bytes.
- Resolution: deterministic poison of that key, emit conflict, and reject dependents referencing poisoned subject.

4. `CF-04 missing_dependency`
- Trigger: op references endpoint/subject not yet admitted.
- Resolution: hold in `PendingSet`; retry in deterministic `op_id` order until fixpoint.
If dependency becomes poisoned, convert to rejection conflict.

5. `CF-05 invariant_violation`
- Trigger: admitted dependencies exist but op violates `INV-S*`, `INV-R*`, `INV-T*`, or `INV-P*`.
- Resolution: reject op and emit conflict; no partial mutation.

6. `CF-06 tx_void_race`
- Trigger: multiple visible void operations targeting same `tx_id`.
- Resolution: effective `voided_at_tx_id = min(void_event_tx_id)`; transaction visibility boundary uses earliest void deterministically.

7. `CF-07 exact_slot_contention`
- Trigger: multiple visible revisions share exact `(core_id, valid_interval)`.
- Resolution: compute deterministic slot winner over candidates that are lifecycle-active and justified at `tx_asof`:
`winner = first(sort(candidates, key=(precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)))`.
Non-winners are shadowed in query projection (not deleted).

8. `CF-08 partial_interval_overlap`
- Trigger: revisions for same `core_id` overlap in valid time but intervals are not identical.
- Resolution: preserve all revisions and emit explicit overlap conflict in `open` state until an admitted interval surgery record resolves the component.

9. `CF-09 interval_surgery_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `apply_interval_surgery` carries `basis_hash` that does not match deterministic recomputation at admission boundary.
- Resolution: reject surgery op and emit conflict; component remains under `CF-08`.

10. `CF-10 migration_path_unsupported`
- Trigger: no admitted active `EpochMigrationSpec` exists for `(source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id)`.
- Resolution: keep source ops quarantined under `CF-02`; emit migration-path conflict with deterministic reason code.

11. `CF-11 migration_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `apply_epoch_migration` plan `source_set_hash` does not match recomputation at admission boundary.
- Resolution: reject migration op; leave all referenced source conflicts open.

12. `CF-12 migrated_projection_mismatch`
- Trigger: same deterministic per-hop projected key (legacy `v0.7` keyspace)
`(target_epoch_id, migration_spec_id, source_op_id)` appears with divergent projected payload bytes.
- Resolution: poison projected key, emit conflict, and block resolution of linked source `CF-02` entries.

13. `CF-13 migration_projection_invariant_violation`
- Trigger: deterministic projection succeeds syntactically but projected op fails dependency or invariant gates in target epoch.
- Resolution: reject projected op, emit conflict linked to `source_op_id`, keep source `CF-02` unresolved.

14. `CF-14 witness_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `derived_from` witness carries `basis_mode`/`witness_basis_keys`/`basis_hash` that do not match deterministic recomputation from premise lineage closure at assertion boundary.
- Resolution: reject relation op, emit conflict, and keep witness excluded from admitted graph.

15. `CF-15 migration_path_resolution_failure`
- Trigger: no eligible acyclic path from `source_epoch_id` to `target_epoch_id` under active `EpochPathPolicy` constraints.
- Resolution: reject affected migration source entries, emit conflict, keep linked `CF-02` entries open.

16. `CF-16 migration_nonconfluent_projection`
- Trigger: same deterministic projection version key `(target_epoch_id, root_source_epoch_id, root_source_op_id, policy_seq)` appears with divergent projected payload bytes.
- Resolution: poison projection version key, emit conflict, and block resolution of linked source `CF-02` entries.

17. `CF-17 migration_policy_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `apply_epoch_migration` carries `path_policy_hash` that differs from recomputed active policy hash at admission boundary.
- Resolution: reject migration op; leave referenced source conflicts open.

18. `CF-18 migration_compaction_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `apply_migration_compaction` `basis_hash` mismatches deterministic recomputation at admission boundary.
- Resolution: reject compaction op; keep dominated/winner metadata unchanged.

19. `CF-19 interval_strategy_policy_basis_mismatch`
- Trigger: `apply_interval_surgery` carries `strategy_policy_hash` that differs from active `IntervalStrategyPolicy` hash for the component claim type at admission boundary.
- Resolution: reject surgery op; keep overlap component under `CF-08` and emit policy-basis conflict.

20. `CF-20 interval_strategy_not_admissible`
- Trigger: referenced `strategy_id` or serialized rank inputs violate the declared `admissibility_profile_id` for the governing interval strategy policy.
- Resolution: reject policy/surgery op deterministically and emit admissibility conflict.

21. `CF-21 interval_strategy_nonconfluent_projection`
- Trigger: same `surgery_projection_key` appears with divergent `segment_plan` payload bytes.

### Convergence Invariants

- `INV-C1`: Every replicated op is identity-addressed by deterministic `op_id` and bound to one `schema_epoch_id`.
- `INV-C2`: Merge is associative, commutative, and idempotent over base keyed sets.
- `INV-C3`: Admission reducer order is deterministic (`op_id ASC`) and independent of network arrival order.
- `INV-C4`: Unknown schema epochs are quarantined; cross-epoch auto-merge is forbidden.
- `INV-C5`: Same-key divergent payloads cannot be silently accepted; they enter poisoned state with conflict record.
- `INV-C6`: Dependency admission is all-or-nothing; pending ops never partially apply.
- `INV-C7`: Effective transaction visibility for voided transactions uses earliest visible void tx boundary.
- `INV-C8`: Exact-slot visibility is single-winner deterministic under `(precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)` over justified lifecycle-active candidates.
- `INV-C9`: Partial interval overlap is conflict-explicit and non-destructive until interval surgery is invoked.
- `INV-C10`: If two replicas hold the same admitted op set and conflict records, all derived outputs (`query_as_of`, `confidence_as_of`, lineage, conflict views) are identical.
- `INV-C11`: For any overlap component with multiple visible surgery records, effective surgery projection is single-winner deterministic under `(policy_seq DESC, tx_id DESC, surgery_id ASC)`.
- `INV-C12`: For fixed `(target_epoch_id, root_source_epoch_id, root_source_op_id, policy_seq)`, projection version key identity is deterministic and migration-attempt order independent.
- `INV-C13`: `apply_epoch_migration` admission is CAS-gated by exact `source_set_hash` and `path_policy_hash`; stale plans are rejected (`CF-11`, `CF-17`).
- `INV-C14`: Source `CF-02` records transition `open -> resolved` only after linked projected ops are admitted without poison/conflict.
- `INV-C15`: Contention and overlap winner ranking uses `precedence_tx_id` (not raw assertion tx) to preserve source chronology through migration.
- `INV-C16`: Witness basis verification and anchor-mass derivation are deterministic; equal admitted op sets cannot disagree on same-rule confidence decomposition outcomes.
- `INV-C17`: Multi-hop path resolution is deterministic for fixed admitted specs/policies and source set; arrival order cannot change chosen path manifests.
- `INV-C18`: Projection confluence is deterministic per projection version key; divergent payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-16` poison).
- `INV-C19`: Effective migrated projection per lineage key is single-winner deterministic under `(policy_seq DESC, precedence_tx_id DESC, projected_version_key ASC)`.
- `INV-C20`: Migration compaction is idempotent and query-invariant; applying equivalent compaction plans in any order yields identical effective query outputs.
- `INV-C21`: Active interval strategy policy selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(policy_seq DESC, interval_policy_id ASC)` for each `claim_type_id`.
- `INV-C22`: `apply_interval_surgery` admission is CAS-gated by both `basis_hash` and `strategy_policy_hash`; stale-policy plans are rejected (`CF-19`).
- `INV-C23`: Interval strategy admissibility verification is deterministic under epoch-bound strategy catalogs and profile definitions; invalid strategies/inputs are rejected (`CF-20`).
- `INV-C24`: Surgery projection confluence is deterministic per `surgery_projection_key`; divergent payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-21` poison).
- `INV-C25`: Active retention policy selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(policy_seq DESC, retention_policy_id ASC)` for each `artifact_class`.
- `INV-C26`: `apply_retention_gc` admission is CAS-gated by exact `retention_policy_hash` and `gc_basis_hash`; stale-policy/stale-basis plans are rejected (`CF-22`, `CF-23`).
- `INV-C27`: Rehydration is deterministic for fixed admitted upstream state and immutable `rehydration_manifest_hash`; hash mismatch is explicit conflict (`CF-25`) rather than silent divergence.
- `INV-C28`: Physical retention/GC is query-invariant for supported artifact classes; equivalent admitted op/conflict sets yield identical logical query outputs regardless of reclaim/rehydrate execution order.
- `INV-C29`: Rule-local bridge-safe aggregation is deterministic for fixed visible witness/premise sets; witness arrival order cannot change inferred confidence.
- `INV-C30`: Base payload retention is semantic-kernel preserving: reclaim operations cannot alter deterministic query/inference inputs over `ClaimRevision`, `EvidenceAtom`, or executable `RuleRecord` kernels.
- `INV-C31`: Base payload reclamation admission is deterministic under visible capsule set and kernel hashes; equal admitted op/conflict sets cannot disagree on reclaim eligibility outcomes (`CF-26`, `CF-27`).
- `INV-C32`: Base payload reclaim/rehydrate/rollback interleavings are query-invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only physical payload residency changes.
- `INV-C33`: Active `CapsuleCachePolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(policy_seq DESC, capsule_cache_policy_id ASC)` for each base `artifact_class`.
- `INV-C34`: Base-class GC and cache-lease transitions are CAS-gated by exact `cache_policy_hash` and deterministic `cache_basis_hash`; stale policy/basis operations are rejected (`CF-28`, `CF-29`).
- `INV-C35`: Cache lease confluence is deterministic for fixed `(capsule_id, policy_seq, lease_seq)`; divergent cache images cannot both be admitted (`CF-31` poison).
- `INV-C36`: Cache warm/evict/rehydrate/rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only latency-tier residency and lease-conflict state may change (`INV-C32` preserved).
- `INV-C37`: Active `CapsuleProfilePolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(policy_seq DESC, capsule_profile_policy_id ASC)` for each base `artifact_class`.
- `INV-C38`: Base capsule/retention/cache/rotation admissions are CAS-gated by exact `profile_policy_hash`; stale profile-policy operations are rejected (`CF-32`).
- `INV-C39`: Capsule profile rotation admission is CAS-gated by exact `rotation_basis_hash`; stale rotation candidate sets are rejected (`CF-33`).
- `INV-C40`: Capsule codec/key admissibility is deterministic under epoch-bound profile catalogs and active policy read-compat tuples; invalid tuples are rejected (`CF-34`).
- `INV-C41`: Rotation confluence is deterministic per `rotation_projection_key`; divergent rebinding payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-35` poison).
- `INV-C42`: Codec/key compatibility retirement is deterministic and pin-safe: removed tuples cannot retire while referenced by visible reclaimed keys or active leases (`CF-36`).
- `INV-C43`: Cache lease, profile rotation, codec/key cutover, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only residency/profile conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C36` preserved).
- `INV-C44`: Active `CapsuleTierPolicyFamily` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(family_seq DESC, capsule_tier_policy_family_id ASC)` for each base `artifact_class`.
- `INV-C45`: Tier-governed retention/cache/profile/assignment admissions are CAS-gated by exact `tier_family_policy_hash` and deterministic `tier_basis_hash`; stale family/basis operations are rejected (`CF-37`, `CF-38`).
- `INV-C46`: Tier assignment admissibility is deterministic under selector/admissibility profiles and visible member-policy references; invalid assignments are rejected (`CF-39`).
- `INV-C47`: Tier cost-bound enforcement is deterministic for fixed candidate set and cost profile; overflow cannot be resolved by local runtime heuristics and is surfaced as explicit conflict (`CF-40`).
- `INV-C48`: Tier assignment confluence is deterministic per `tier_projection_key`; divergent assignment payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-41` poison).
- `INV-C49`: Tier-family cutover, assignment, lease, rotation, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only latency/cost conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C43` preserved).
- `INV-C50`: Active `CapsuleTierTelemetryPolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(telemetry_seq DESC, capsule_tier_telemetry_policy_id ASC)` for each active `(artifact_class, family_seq)`.
- `INV-C51`: Tier telemetry and utility admissions are CAS-gated by exact `tier_telemetry_policy_hash`, `telemetry_basis_hash`, and `utility_basis_hash`; stale policy/basis operations are rejected (`CF-42`, `CF-43`).
- `INV-C52`: Telemetry admissibility and dedupe are deterministic under active window/admissibility profiles; invalid telemetry records are rejected (`CF-44`).
- `INV-C53`: Tier utility confluence is deterministic per `utility_projection_key`; divergent utility payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-45` poison).
- `INV-C54`: Anti-gaming normalization is deterministic: per-observer cap, dominant-share cap, and distinct-observer floor produce identical utility outcomes across replicas; violations are rejected (`CF-46`).
- `INV-C55`: Telemetry ingestion, utility projection, tier assignment, lease, rotation, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational latency/cost/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C49` preserved).
- `INV-C56`: Active `CapsuleGlobalBudgetPolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(budget_seq DESC, capsule_global_budget_policy_id ASC)` for each `budget_domain_id`.
- `INV-C57`: Global budget arbitration and budget-governed retention/tier/cache/profile admissions are CAS-gated by exact `global_budget_policy_hash` and `global_budget_basis_hash`; stale policy/basis operations are rejected (`CF-47`, `CF-48`).
- `INV-C58`: Global budget fair-share and SLA-floor admissibility is deterministic under active profile IDs and canonical class tuples; invalid payloads are rejected (`CF-49`).
- `INV-C59`: Global budget arbitration confluence is deterministic per `global_budget_projection_key`; divergent allocation payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-50` poison).
- `INV-C60`: Global budget arbitration, telemetry utility, tier assignment, cache lease, profile rotation, retention reclaim, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational allocation/cost/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C55` preserved).
- `INV-C61`: Active `CapsuleGlobalBudgetMemoryPolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(memory_policy_seq DESC, capsule_global_budget_memory_policy_id ASC)` for each `budget_domain_id`.
- `INV-C62`: Global budget memory and memory-aware arbitration admissions are CAS-gated by exact `global_budget_memory_policy_hash` and `global_budget_memory_basis_hash`; stale policy/basis operations are rejected (`CF-52`, `CF-53`).
- `INV-C63`: Deficit carryover, burst-credit decay, and SLA-debt transitions are deterministic under active memory profiles/caps; invalid payloads/transitions are rejected (`CF-54`, `CF-56`).
- `INV-C64`: Long-horizon memory confluence is deterministic per `memory_projection_key`; divergent memory payloads cannot both be admitted (`CF-55` poison).
- `INV-C65`: Global budget memory, arbitration, telemetry utility, tier assignment, cache lease, profile rotation, retention reclaim, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational allocation/cost/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C60` preserved).
- `INV-C66`: Active `CapsuleUtilizationAttestationPolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(utilization_policy_seq DESC, capsule_utilization_attestation_policy_id ASC)` for each `budget_domain_id`.

- `INV-C68`: Utilization attestation admissibility is deterministic under active window/quorum/reconciliation profiles; invalid attester proofs, window tuples, or counter serializations are rejected (`CF-59`).
