- `INV-C70`: Utilization attestation/reconciliation, global budget memory/arbitration, telemetry utility, tier assignment, cache lease, profile rotation, retention reclaim, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational allocation/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C65` preserved).
- `INV-C71`: Active `CapsuleAttesterTrustPolicy` selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(attester_policy_seq DESC, capsule_attester_trust_policy_id ASC)` for each `budget_domain_id`.

- `INV-C75`: Attester-set rotation/continuity, utilization attestation/reconciliation, global budget memory/arbitration, tier assignment, cache lease, profile rotation, retention reclaim, rehydrate, and rollback interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational trust/allocation/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C70` preserved).

- `INV-C244`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge projection confluence is deterministic per `coupling_amnesty_closure_relapse_escrow_window_projection_key`, `coupling_thaw_reentry_ladder_rebalance_projection_key`, and `coupling_redemption_decay_probation_settlement_projection_key`; divergent payloads cannot co-exist (`CF-235`).
- `INV-C245`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge + settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality + settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation + settlement-discharge-finality-closure + settlement-discharge-finality + settlement-discharge + closure-finalization + continuity + closure + terminal + relapse + finality + settlement + upstream lifecycle/governance interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational privacy/trust/allocation/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C240` preserved).
- `INV-C246`: Active `` selection is deterministic per `budget_domain_id` under `(settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_policy_seq DESC, policy_id ASC)`.
- `INV-C247`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization records and settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-governed downstream admissions are CAS-gated by exact `attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_evidence_integrity_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_handoff_portfolio_resilience_family_settlement_finality_relapse_terminal_closure_continuity_closure_finalization_settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_policy_hash`, `coupling_relapse_escrow_exhaustion_release_basis_hash`, `coupling_thaw_reentry_rebalance_deadband_cutover_basis_hash`, and `coupling_probation_settlement_restitution_rollforward_basis_hash`; stale operations reject (`CF-237`, `CF-238`).
- `INV-C248`: Relapse-escrow-exhaustion-release, thaw-reentry-rebalance-deadband-cutover, and probation-settlement-restitution-rollforward tuples are deterministic under active settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization policy; invalid payloads reject (`CF-239`).
- `INV-C249`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization projection confluence is deterministic per `coupling_relapse_escrow_exhaustion_release_projection_key`, `coupling_thaw_reentry_rebalance_deadband_cutover_projection_key`, and `coupling_probation_settlement_restitution_rollforward_projection_key`; divergent payloads cannot co-exist (`CF-240`).
- `INV-C250`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization + settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge + settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality + settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation + settlement-discharge-finality-closure + settlement-discharge-finality + settlement-discharge + closure-finalization + continuity + closure + terminal + relapse + finality + settlement + upstream lifecycle/governance interleavings are logical-query invariant for fixed admitted op/conflict sets; only operational privacy/trust/allocation/conflict surfaces may change (`INV-C245` preserved).
- `INV-C251`: Active `` selection is deterministic per `budget_domain_id` under `(settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_finality_policy_seq DESC, policy_id ASC)`.
- `INV-C252`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality records and settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality-governed downstream admissions are CAS-gated by exact `attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_evidence_integrity_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_handoff_portfolio_resilience_family_settlement_finality_relapse_terminal_closure_continuity_closure_finalization_settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_finality_policy_hash`, `coupling_exhaustion_release_amnesty_restitution_closure_basis_hash`, `coupling_rebalance_deadband_hysteresis_quarantine_exit_cutover_basis_hash`, and `coupling_restitution_rollforward_debt_recertification_basis_hash`; stale operations reject (`CF-242`, `CF-243`).
- `INV-C253`: Exhaustion-release-amnesty-restitution-closure, rebalance-deadband-hysteresis-quarantine-exit-cutover, and restitution-rollforward-debt-recertification tuples are deterministic under active settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality policy; invalid payloads reject (`CF-244`).
- `INV-C254`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality projection confluence is deterministic per `coupling_exhaustion_release_amnesty_restitution_closure_projection_key`, `coupling_rebalance_deadband_hysteresis_quarantine_exit_cutover_projection_key`, and `coupling_restitution_rollforward_debt_recertification_projection_key`; divergent payloads cannot co-exist (`CF-245`).

- `INV-C256`: Active `` selection is deterministic per `budget_domain_id` under `(settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_finality_discharge_policy_seq DESC, policy_id ASC)`.
- `INV-C257`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality-discharge records and settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality-discharge-governed downstream admissions are CAS-gated by exact `attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_evidence_integrity_trust_calibration_portfolio_stability_family_handoff_portfolio_resilience_family_settlement_finality_relapse_terminal_closure_continuity_closure_finalization_settlement_discharge_finality_closure_reconciliation_finality_discharge_stabilization_finality_discharge_policy_hash`, `coupling_amnesty_restitution_closure_debt_sunset_exhaustion_basis_hash`, `coupling_hysteresis_quarantine_exit_deadband_collapse_cutover_basis_hash`, and `coupling_debt_recertification_restitution_terminalization_basis_hash`; stale operations reject (`CF-247`, `CF-248`).
- `INV-C258`: Amnesty-restitution-closure-debt-sunset-exhaustion, hysteresis-quarantine-exit-deadband-collapse-cutover, and debt-recertification-restitution-terminalization tuples are deterministic under active settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality-discharge policy; invalid payloads reject (`CF-249`).
- `INV-C259`: Settlement-discharge-finality-closure-reconciliation-finality-discharge-stabilization-finality-discharge projection confluence is deterministic per `coupling_amnesty_restitution_closure_debt_sunset_exhaustion_projection_key`, `coupling_hysteresis_quarantine_exit_deadband_collapse_cutover_projection_key`, and `coupling_debt_recertification_restitution_terminalization_projection_key`; divergent payloads cannot co-exist (`CF-250`).

### Deterministic Sync Operations

1. `merge_replica_delta(remote_ops, tx_asof) -> merge_report`
- Union `remote_ops` into local `OpSet`.
- Reduce in sorted `op_id` order with precedence:
`schema_epoch_gate -> id_integrity_gate -> dependency_gate -> invariant_gate -> admission`.
- Re-run `PendingSet` promotion to fixpoint.
- Recompute transaction visibility, slot contention projection, interval-surgery projection, migration cutover/compaction views, retention + cache-lease + profile-rotation + telemetry/utility + utilization-attestation/reconciliation + global-budget-memory + global-budget-arbitration + attester-adjudication/appeal-finality + attester-portability/review-mux + attester-portability-disclosure/review-attestation + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-calibration + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-objective/hysteresis + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-integrity + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality-relapse + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality-relapse-terminal + attester-disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality-relapse-terminal-closure + tier-assignment views, justification/confidence, and conflict records.
- Return canonical report:
`admitted_op_ids`, `rejected_op_ids`, `pending_op_ids`, `conflict_ids` (all sorted ascending).

2. `resolve_exact_slot(core_id, valid_interval, tx_asof) -> winner_revision_id | null`
- Gather visible revisions with identical slot.
- Keep only lifecycle-active + justified candidates.
- Return deterministic winner by `(precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)`; return `null` if none.

3. `query_conflicts(subject_ref, tx_asof) -> ordered_conflicts`
- Return visible conflict records touching `subject_ref`, ordered by `(first_seen_tx_id ASC, conflict_id ASC)`.

## Interval Surgery Semantics (v0.6 + v0.10 strategy governance)

### Design Intent

`CF-08` overlap is intentionally non-destructive until an explicit surgery record is admitted.
Surgery is projection-level: base revisions and lineage relations remain immutable.
`v0.10` adds deterministic multi-strategy governance, admissibility constraints, and policy cutover rules.

### Strategy Governance and Policy Cutover

For each `claim_type_id`, interval strategy policy is selected at `tx_asof` by:
`first(sort(visible_policies, key=(policy_seq DESC, interval_policy_id ASC)))`.

Policy payload fields:
- `claim_type_id`
- `policy_seq`
- `strategy_id`
- `admissibility_profile_id`
- `policy_hash`

Current strategy families:
- `tx_recency_rev_tiebreak_v1`:
`rank_tuple = (precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)`.
- `confidence_then_precedence_v1`:
`rank_tuple = (confidence_bp_plan DESC, precedence_tx_id DESC, revision_id ASC)`.

Current admissibility profile:
- `deterministic_interval_rank_inputs_v1`:
every rank tuple element must be canonicalized from deterministic functions over visible state at `plan_tx_asof`; free-form model outputs are forbidden.

### Interval Component and Plan Canonicalization

For fixed `(core_id, tx_asof)`, define overlap graph over visible lifecycle-active and justified revisions:
edge exists when intervals overlap and are not identical.
Each connected component is an `IntervalComponent`.

- `component_id = H(ns_icomp, core_id, sorted(revision_ids))`.
- `boundary_set = sorted_unique(all starts + ends of component intervals)`.
- Atomic segments:
for adjacent boundaries `b_i < b_{i+1}`, create half-open segment `[b_i, b_{i+1})`.
- For each segment, compute `cover_set` of revisions containing that segment.
- Deterministic candidate ranking per segment:
compute canonical `rank_tuple` by selected `strategy_id` at `plan_tx_asof`,
then `sort(cover_set, key=(rank_tuple DESC/ASC as declared, revision_id ASC))`.
- Coalesce adjacent segments with identical ranked candidate lists (merge canonicalization).

### IntervalSurgeryRecord

`IntervalSurgeryRecord` payload:
- `core_id`
- `claim_type_id`
- `component_id`
- `basis_hash`
- `strategy_id`
- `policy_seq`
- `strategy_policy_hash`
- `strategy_basis_hash`
- `surgery_projection_key`
- `plan_tx_asof`
- `segment_plan`: ordered list of
`(segment_interval, candidate_revision_ids_ranked, candidate_rank_tuples)`
- `tx_id`

`basis_hash = H(ns_ibasis, component_id, sorted(tuple(revision_id, valid_interval, precedence_tx_id)))`
computed at planning snapshot `tx_asof`.
`strategy_policy_hash = H(ns_isph, claim_type_id, policy_seq, strategy_id, admissibility_profile_id, policy_hash)`.
`strategy_basis_hash = H(ns_isbasis, strategy_id, plan_tx_asof, sorted(tuple(segment_interval, revision_id, rank_tuple_bytes)))`.
`surgery_projection_key = H(ns_isproj, core_id, component_id, policy_seq, strategy_id, basis_hash, plan_tx_asof)`.
Admission requires compare-and-swap match on `basis_hash` and `strategy_policy_hash`, plus admissibility verification and projection-confluence checks.

### Split, Clip, Merge Interpretation

- `split`: one revision appears in multiple disjoint `segment_interval`s.
- `clip`: revision is absent from a segment it originally covered because it is outranked in ranked candidates.
- `merge`: adjacent equivalent segments are coalesced during plan canonicalization.

### Interval Invariants

- `INV-I1`: Interval surgery never mutates/deletes `ClaimRevision`, `RevisionStatusEvent`, `RelationRecord`, or `EvidenceAtom`.
- `INV-I2`: `segment_plan` intervals are normalized half-open intervals with strictly increasing boundaries.
- `INV-I3`: `segment_plan` intervals are non-overlapping and collectively contained in the targeted component union.
- `INV-I4`: `candidate_revision_ids_ranked` is sorted unique and each member covers the segment interval.
- `INV-I5`: Candidate ranking key is deterministic and strategy-bound by canonical `rank_tuple` serialization.
- `INV-I6`: Surgery admission is conditional on exact `basis_hash` match; mismatch is `CF-09`.
- `INV-I7`: Effective surgery for one `(core_id, component_id)` is deterministic by `(policy_seq DESC, tx_id DESC, surgery_id ASC)` across visible, unpoisoned surgery records.
- `INV-I8`: Query-time winner for a segment is first candidate that is lifecycle-active and justified at `tx_asof`; fallback to next candidate is deterministic.
- `INV-I9`: Voiding surgery transaction cleanly removes projection effect without changing base lineage closure.
- `INV-I10`: Every surgery plan binds immutable `claim_type_id`, `policy_seq`, `strategy_id`, `plan_tx_asof`, and `strategy_policy_hash`.
- `INV-I11`: Strategy admissibility is enforced against the active policy profile; non-admissible strategies/inputs are rejected (`CF-20`).
- `INV-I12`: `apply_interval_surgery` is policy-CAS gated by exact `strategy_policy_hash`; mismatch yields `CF-19`.
- `INV-I13`: `strategy_basis_hash` is deterministic for fixed `(strategy_id, plan_tx_asof, component snapshot)` and replay-stable across replicas.
- `INV-I14`: For one `surgery_projection_key`, divergent `segment_plan` payload bytes cannot both be admitted; mismatch is `CF-21` poison.
- `INV-I15`: Policy cutover is non-retroactive:
higher-`policy_seq` surgeries affect queries only for `tx_asof` where their asserting transaction is visible.
- `INV-I16`: For fixed admitted surgery set and policy records, `resolve_interval_component` output is replica-stable independent of op arrival order.
- `INV-I17`: Physical reclamation of superseded surgery projections is allowed only through retention GC with immutable rehydration manifests and preserved query determinism (`INV-G*`).
- `INV-I18`: Rehydration of reclaimed surgery projections is deterministic from retained planning basis fields and hash-verified; mismatch is `CF-25`.

### Deterministic Interval Surgery Operations

1. `upsert_interval_strategy_policy(policy_record, tx_id) -> interval_policy_id | rejection`
- Validate `strategy_id` and `admissibility_profile_id` against epoch strategy catalog.
- Enforce uniqueness for `(claim_type_id, policy_seq)` (divergent duplicates rejected as `CF-05` invariant violation).
- Admit immutable policy record for future surgery planning.

2. `plan_interval_surgery(core_id, tx_asof) -> ordered plans`
- Enumerate open `CF-08` overlap components for `core_id`.
- Resolve active `IntervalStrategyPolicy` for `core_id` claim type under deterministic policy selection.
- If policy missing or strategy/profile unsupported: emit `CF-20` and return rejection for affected components.
- For each component, compute canonical `segment_plan`, `basis_hash`, `strategy_policy_hash`, `strategy_basis_hash`, and `surgery_projection_key`.
- Return plans ordered by `component_id ASC`.

3. `apply_interval_surgery(plan, tx_id) -> surgery_id | rejection`
- Recompute component and `basis_hash` at admission boundary.
- If mismatch: reject and emit `CF-09`.
- Recompute active interval strategy policy hash for component claim type; mismatch -> `CF-19`.
- Verify plan rank inputs against `admissibility_profile_id`; violation -> `CF-20`.
- Recompute `strategy_basis_hash`; mismatch -> `CF-20`.
- Enforce projection confluence by `surgery_projection_key`; divergent payload -> `CF-21`.
- If all checks pass: admit `IntervalSurgeryRecord`, set affected `CF-08` records to `resolved` with `chosen_winner=surgery_id`.

4. `resolve_interval_component(core_id, valid_at, tx_asof) -> revision_id | null | ambiguous`
- Find overlap component covering `valid_at`.
- If none: return `null` and caller keeps normal non-overlap path.
- If component exists and no active surgery: return `ambiguous`.
- If active surgery exists: pick winner surgery via `INV-I7`, locate segment containing `valid_at`, return first candidate passing lifecycle+justification at `tx_asof`; return `null` if none.

5. `query_interval_strategy_state(claim_type_id, tx_asof) -> ordered_strategy_view`
- Return canonical strategy-governance state:
visible policies, open/resolved `CF-08` components, surgery records by `policy_seq`, and unresolved strategy conflicts (`CF-19..CF-21`),
ordered by `(policy_seq DESC, tx_id ASC, interval_policy_id ASC, surgery_id ASC)`.

## Cross-Epoch Migration Semantics (v0.7 + v0.9)

### Design Intent

`CF-02` quarantine prevents silent split-brain but blocks useful data flow when epochs evolve.
`v0.7` introduced deterministic single-hop migration admission.
`v0.9` extends migration to deterministic multi-hop composition with explicit path policy cutovers and metadata-only compaction.

### Migration Graph, Root Lineage, and Projection Keys

Active migration specs form a directed epoch graph:
- Node: `schema_epoch_id`.
- Edge: admitted `EpochMigrationSpec(source_epoch_id -> target_epoch_id)`.
- Edge projector is pure and immutable by `migration_spec_id`.

Every migration candidate carries immutable root lineage:
- `root_source_epoch_id`
- `root_source_op_id`

Root lineage rules:
- For quarantined source ops: root lineage is self (`source_epoch_id`, `source_op_id`).
- For already migrated ops used as upstream input: reuse stored root lineage unchanged.

Path and projection identity:
- `path_fingerprint = H(ns_mpath, ordered(migration_spec_id))`.
- `projection_lineage_key = H(ns_mlineage, target_epoch_id, root_source_epoch_id, root_source_op_id)`.
- `projected_version_key = H(ns_mver, target_epoch_id, root_source_epoch_id, root_source_op_id, policy_seq)`.

### Path Selection and Cutover Precedence

For target epoch `E_t`, one active `EpochPathPolicy` is selected at `tx_asof` by:
`max(policy_seq, path_policy_id ASC)` among lifecycle-visible policy records for `E_t`.
If no visible policy exists, migration planning emits `CF-10`.

Current selector family (`shortest_path_spec_lex_v1`):
1. Enumerate eligible simple acyclic paths from `source_epoch_id` to `target_epoch_id`
that satisfy policy source constraints.
2. Rank paths by deterministic key `(hop_count ASC, path_fingerprint ASC)`.
3. Choose first ranked path; if none, emit `CF-15`.

Plan snapshot for fixed `(source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, tx_asof, limit)`:
- Enumerate open `CF-02` source ops sorted by `op_id ASC`.
- Select prefix of size `limit`.
- Compute:
`source_set_hash = H(ns_msrc, source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, sorted(tuple(source_op_id, source_payload_hash)))`.
- Resolve active policy and compute:
`path_policy_hash = H(ns_mphash, path_policy_id, policy_seq, selector_id, selector_hash, source_epoch_allowlist_hash)`.
- Resolve chosen path per source op and compute:
`path_set_hash = H(ns_mpathset, sorted(tuple(source_op_id, policy_seq, hop_count, path_fingerprint)))`.
- Compose path projector functions in path order and compute terminal projection digest:
`projection_hash = H(ns_mproj, sorted(tuple(source_op_id, projected_kind, projected_subject_id, projected_payload_hash, projected_version_key)))`.

Projection visibility and chronology:
- Projected ops are asserted in local `tx_id = migration_tx_id` (non-retroactive visibility).
- If projected op creates/updates revision payload, set `origin_tx_id = root_source_tx_id`.
- Query ranking uses `precedence_tx_id`, preserving source chronology without pre-migration visibility.

Effective projection cutover for one `projection_lineage_key`:
- Consider visible, unpoisoned projected versions.
- Deterministic winner:
`first(sort(candidates, key=(policy_seq DESC, precedence_tx_id DESC, projected_version_key ASC)))`.

### Compaction Semantics (Metadata-Only)

Compaction does not delete or rewrite base ops/revisions.
It only marks dominated projected versions as compacted for audit/index hygiene.

For fixed `(target_epoch_id, policy_seq, tx_asof, limit)`:
- Enumerate lineage keys with multiple visible projected versions sorted by `projection_lineage_key ASC`.
- For each lineage key, compute winner by cutover rule and dominated set (all other visible versions).
- Build deterministic plan prefix of size `limit`.
- Compute:
`compaction_basis_hash = H(ns_mcbasis, target_epoch_id, policy_seq, sorted(tuple(projection_lineage_key, winner_projected_version_key, dominated_projected_version_key)))`.

Admission:
- `apply_migration_compaction` recomputes `compaction_basis_hash`.
- Mismatch emits `CF-18`.
- Match admits `MigrationCompactionRecord` and marks dominated versions as `compacted=true` in migration metadata views only.

### Migration Invariants

- `INV-M1`: Quarantined source ops are immutable; migration never rewrites source payload bytes.
- `INV-M2`: Path specs are deterministic and immutable by `migration_spec_id`.
- `INV-M3`: Projection output per hop is pure function of `(migration_spec_id, input_op_bytes)`.
- `INV-M4`: Active migration spec graph must be acyclic; cycle-inducing spec/path activation is rejected (`CF-15`).
- `INV-M5`: Root lineage (`root_source_epoch_id`, `root_source_op_id`) is immutable across hops.
- `INV-M6`: `projected_version_key` is deterministic and unique per `(target_epoch_id, root_source_epoch_id, root_source_op_id, policy_seq)`.
- `INV-M7`: `apply_epoch_migration` is CAS-gated by exact `source_set_hash`; mismatch yields `CF-11`.
- `INV-M8`: `apply_epoch_migration` is policy-CAS-gated by exact `path_policy_hash`; mismatch yields `CF-17`.
- `INV-M9`: Migration is forward-visible only:
projected ops are invisible for `tx_asof < migration_tx_id` regardless of `origin_tx_id`.
- `INV-M10`: If `origin_tx_id` is present, ranking uses it via `precedence_tx_id`; migration tx recency cannot reorder source chronology.
- `INV-M11`: Source `CF-02` conflicts resolve only when linked projected versions are admitted and unpoisoned; otherwise remain open with linked conflict class.
- `INV-M12`: For one projected version key, divergent payload bytes cannot both be admitted; mismatch is `CF-16` poison.
- `INV-M13`: Effective lineage winner is deterministic under `(policy_seq DESC, precedence_tx_id DESC, projected_version_key ASC)`.
- `INV-M14`: Voiding migration transaction hides projected outputs from that batch and deterministically reopens linked source `CF-02` records where no alternate admitted projection remains.
- `INV-M15`: Compaction is metadata-only and query-invariant; it cannot change visible fact winners.
- `INV-M16`: `apply_migration_compaction` is CAS-gated by exact `compaction_basis_hash`; mismatch yields `CF-18`.
- `INV-M17`: Compaction is idempotent: replaying equivalent plans cannot change winners or conflict states.
- `INV-M18`: Voiding compaction transaction restores pre-compaction metadata visibility without changing base fact/query outputs.
- `INV-M19`: For fixed `(target_epoch_id, policy_seq)`, at most one visible policy payload may exist; conflicting duplicates are rejected as invariant violations (`CF-05`).
- `INV-M20`: Active path policy selection is deterministic at `tx_asof` under `(policy_seq DESC, path_policy_id ASC)`.
- `INV-M21`: Physical reclamation of dominated projected versions is allowed only through admitted retention GC records carrying immutable rehydration manifests and payload hashes.
- `INV-M22`: Rehydration of reclaimed projected versions from retained source lineage/specs is deterministic and hash-verifiable; mismatch is surfaced as `CF-25`.

### Deterministic Migration Operations

1. `upsert_epoch_path_policy(policy_record, tx_id) -> path_policy_id | rejection`
- Validate selector family and source constraints.
- Enforce `INV-M19` uniqueness for `(target_epoch_id, policy_seq)`.
- Admit immutable policy record for future migration planning.

2. `plan_epoch_migration(source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, tx_asof, limit) -> migration_plan | rejection`
- Require at least one admitted migration-spec path family for `(source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id)`; if none emit `CF-10`.
- Resolve eligible acyclic paths under active policy; if none remain, emit `CF-15`.
- Build canonical source set and hashes (`source_set_hash`, `path_policy_hash`, `path_set_hash`, `projection_hash`).
- Return immutable plan payload:
`source_op_ids`, `path_manifest`, `source_set_hash`, `path_policy_hash`, `path_set_hash`, `projection_hash`, `non_projectable_source_op_ids` (sorted).

3. `apply_epoch_migration(migration_plan, tx_id) -> migration_id | rejection`
- Recompute `source_set_hash`; mismatch -> `CF-11`.
- Recompute `path_policy_hash`; mismatch -> `CF-17`.
- Recompute path manifest and deterministic projections.
- If any projected version key payload mismatch appears for pre-existing key: emit `CF-16`, keep linked source conflicts open.
- Admit `EpochMigrationRecord`.
- Materialize projected ops under target epoch through normal admission gates in `source_op_id ASC` order.
- For projected revisions, stamp `origin_tx_id = root_source_tx_id`.
- Resolve linked source `CF-02` conflicts only for successful, unpoisoned projected admissions; keep others open with `CF-13` where applicable.

4. `plan_migration_compaction(target_epoch_id, policy_seq, tx_asof, limit) -> compaction_plan | empty`
- Enumerate deterministic winner/dominated sets for lineage keys with multiple versions.
- Return immutable plan with sorted lineage tuples and `compaction_basis_hash`.

5. `apply_migration_compaction(compaction_plan, tx_id) -> compaction_id | rejection`
- Recompute `compaction_basis_hash` at admission boundary.
- If mismatch: reject and emit `CF-18`.
- If match: admit `MigrationCompactionRecord` and set `compacted=true` for dominated projected versions in migration metadata views.

6. `query_migration_state(source_epoch_id, target_epoch_id, tx_asof) -> ordered_migration_view`
- Return canonical migration audit view:
active path policy, active specs, open/closed `CF-02` counts, migration records, compaction records, and unresolved source op IDs,
ordered by `(tx_id ASC, migration_id ASC, compaction_id ASC, source_op_id ASC)`.

## Physical Retention and Garbage-Collection Semantics (v0.11 + v0.13 base expansion + v0.14 cache governance + v0.15 profile governance + v0.16 tier families + v0.17 telemetry utility governance + v0.18 global budget arbitration + v0.19 long-horizon budget memory + v0.20 utilization attestation/reconciliation + v0.21 attester trust rotation/cutover + v0.22 attester accountability memory + v0.23 signal federation rehabilitation memory + v0.32 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling memory + v0.33 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile memory + v0.34 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity memory + v0.35 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration memory + v0.36 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio memory + v0.37 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability memory + v0.38 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family memory + v0.39 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff memory + v0.40 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio memory + v0.41 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience memory + v0.42 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience family settlement finality memory + v0.45 disclosure lifecycle signal federation rehabilitation objective coupling profile evidence-integrity trust-calibration portfolio stability family handoff portfolio resilience family settlement finality relapse memory)

### Design Intent

Migration compaction and surgery cutovers leave dominated projection payloads.
Base-record payloads (`ClaimRevision`, `EvidenceAtom`, `RuleRecord`) can also dominate storage but must not break deterministic query/inference semantics.

`v0.11` introduced deterministic retention for derived projections.
`v0.13` extends retention to canonical base payload classes by separating:
- immutable semantic kernels (always retained, never reclaimable),
- reclaimable payload surfaces (physically reclaimable only with deterministic capsule-backed rehydration proofs).
`v0.14` adds deterministic capsule cache governance so reclaimed base payload reads can satisfy bounded-latency contracts without introducing query/result nondeterminism.
`v0.15` adds deterministic capsule profile governance so codec cutovers and key rotations are policy-sequenced, replay-stable, and confluence-protected without weakening logical query invariance.
`v0.16` adds deterministic workload-tier policy families that bind reclaimed base keys to tier-scoped cache/profile member policies with explicit admissibility and bounded-cost placement constraints.
`v0.17` adds deterministic telemetry-backed tier utility governance so utility signals are derived from replicated telemetry windows with anti-gaming normalization, never runtime-local counters.
`v0.18` adds deterministic cross-artifact-class global budget arbitration so class-level tier placement and reclamation consume globally bounded resources under explicit fair-share and SLA-floor precedence, never runtime-local contention heuristics.
`v0.19` adds deterministic long-horizon global budget memory so deficits, burst credits, and SLA debt evolve by replicated decay/carryover equations with rollback-safe precedence, never runtime-local history.
`v0.20` adds deterministic utilization attestation and late-window reconciliation so memory transitions are derived from attested/reconciled canonical window inputs with explicit quorum/admissibility guards, never runtime-local counters.
`v0.21` adds deterministic attester-set rotation + trust-tiered quorum cutover governance so attester roster/key churn and historical proof continuity are represented as replicated state with explicit cutover guards, never runtime-local trust snapshots.
`v0.22` adds deterministic attester-accountability memory so slashing, reputation decay, and reinstatement evolve through replicated lineage with explicit policy/basis/transition guards, never runtime-local trust penalties.
`v0.23` adds deterministic cross-domain fault adjudication and appeal-finality memory so only finalized dispute verdict lineage can drive accountability transitions.
`v0.24` adds deterministic adjudication portability memory so jurisdiction weighting, evidence-envelope normalization, and appeal-review multiplexing are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local adapter behavior.
`v0.25` adds deterministic portability disclosure memory so redaction commitments, selective reveal proofs, and review attestations are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local disclosure adapters.
`v0.26` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle memory so reveal-budget replenishment, disclosure revocation, and commitment-scheme agility are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local replenishment/revocation/rekey toggles.
`v0.27` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle calibration memory so adaptive replenishment envelopes, revocation granularity classes, and commitment compatibility matrices are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local profile selectors or local compatibility registries.
`v0.28` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle objective memory so privacy/fairness/cost weighting and selector-family hysteresis cutovers are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local objective scorers.
`v0.29` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-integrity memory so objective evidence attestations, lag-compensated normalization, and anti-manipulation verdicts are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local telemetry adapters.
`v0.30` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation memory so cross-domain objective-signal federation attestations, observer-diversity escrow, and stale-feed quarantine transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local federation adapters.
`v0.31` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation memory so quarantine-release proofs, escrow-unlock fairness updates, and cross-domain clock-skew compensation transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local remediation adapters.
`v0.32` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling memory so rehabilitation-triggered selector clamps, fairness-preserving unlock backpressure, and skew-cutover hysteresis transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local control loops.
`v0.33` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile memory so bounded profile portfolio selection, family upgrades, and non-regressive fallback transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local profile registries.
`v0.34` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity memory so upgrade/fallback signal attestation, stale-signal tolerance bounds, and anti-regression proof verification are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local telemetry/proof adapters.
`v0.35` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration memory so multi-attester evidence weighting, proof-expiry cutovers, and dispute-class adaptive tolerance bands are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local trust-score or TTL adapters.
`v0.36` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio memory so bounded weighting-family selection, proof-expiry debt carryover, and cross-dispute fairness caps are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local portfolio schedulers or debt ledgers.
`v0.37` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability memory so portfolio debt amortization ladders, fairness-cap rebound controls, and hysteresis freeze semantics are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local debt amortizers or rebound timers.
`v0.38` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family memory so amortization-ladder portfolio selection, freeze liftoff cutovers, and rebound fallback semantics are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local family catalogs or liftoff controllers.
`v0.39` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff memory so freeze-liftoff debt handoff attestations, rebound-fallback forgiveness-ledger updates, and family-proof carryforward bounds are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local debt/proof cache interpreters.
`v0.40` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio memory so forgiveness-ledger replenishment, carryforward expiry-cliff control, and debt-handoff quorum-degradation updates are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local replenishment/quorum/proof-cliff interpreters.
`v0.41` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience memory so replenishment debt carryforward, expiry-cliff smoothing cutovers, and quorum-recovery probation transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local carryforward/smoothing/probation controllers.
`v0.42` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family memory so debt-carryforward ladder portfolio selection, smoothing-cutover hysteresis-class transitions, and probation-exit restitution transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local ladder/hysteresis/restitution controllers.
`v0.43` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement memory so restitution clawback-window closure, ladder-demotion quarantine progression, and hysteresis debt-cooling cutovers are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local clawback/quarantine/cooling controllers.
`v0.44` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality memory so clawback-appeal escrow closure, ladder-demotion quarantine-release quorum transitions, and debt-cooling reentry cutovers are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local appeal/quorum/reentry controllers.

`v0.45` adds deterministic disclosure lifecycle signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-trust-calibration-portfolio-stability-family-handoff-portfolio-resilience-family-settlement-finality-relapse memory so escrow-closure appeal-reopen constraints, quarantine-release quorum relapse containment, and debt-cooling reentry rebaseline transitions are replicated lineage transitions, never runtime-local reopen/containment/rebaseline controllers.

Retention GC remains logically non-destructive:
- No `ClaimCore`, relation, conflict, or lifecycle record is deleted.
- Base semantic kernels remain visible and immutable after payload reclaim.
- Every reclaimed artifact keeps immutable audit spine:
`gc_artifact_key`, `artifact_payload_hash`, `rehydration_manifest_hash`, reclaim tx metadata, and optional `semantic_kernel_hash`.
- Every bounded-latency base artifact additionally carries immutable cache-governance spine:
`cache_policy_hash`, `cache_basis_hash`, and pinned `capsule_id` lease lineage.
- Every codec/key-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable profile-governance spine:
`profile_policy_hash`, capsule tuple `(codec_profile_id, key_epoch_seq)`, and optional rotation lineage.
- Every workload-tier-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable tier-governance spine:
`tier_family_policy_hash`, `tier_basis_hash`, and effective `tier_id` assignment lineage.
- Every telemetry-backed utility artifact additionally carries immutable telemetry spine:
`tier_telemetry_policy_hash`, `telemetry_basis_hash`, `utility_basis_hash`, and `utility_projection_key` lineage.
- Every global-budget-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable allocation spine:
`global_budget_policy_hash`, `global_budget_basis_hash`, `arbitration_seq`, and class envelope lineage.
- Every long-horizon global-budget-memory-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable memory spine:
`global_budget_memory_policy_hash`, `global_budget_memory_basis_hash`, `memory_seq`, and per-class debt/credit lineage.
- Every utilization-attested base artifact additionally carries immutable attester-trust spine:
`attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_set_basis_hash`, `attester_continuity_basis_hash`, `rotation_seq`, and `continuity_seq`.
- Every accountability-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable attester-accountability spine:
`attester_accountability_policy_hash`, `accountability_basis_hash`, `reinstatement_basis_hash`, `accountability_seq`, and `reinstatement_seq`.
- Every adjudication-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable attester-adjudication spine:
`attester_adjudication_policy_hash`, `adjudication_basis_hash`, `appeal_basis_hash`, `adjudication_seq`, and `appeal_seq`.
- Every adjudication-portability-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable portability spine:
`attester_adjudication_portability_policy_hash`, `portability_basis_hash`, `review_mux_basis_hash`, `portability_seq`, and `review_mux_seq`.
- Every portability-disclosure-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable disclosure spine:
`attester_portability_disclosure_policy_hash`, `disclosure_basis_hash`, `review_attestation_basis_hash`, `disclosure_seq`, and `review_attestation_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-calibration-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable calibration spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_calibration_policy_hash`, `replenishment_envelope_basis_hash`, `revocation_granularity_basis_hash`, `compatibility_matrix_basis_hash`, `replenishment_envelope_seq`, `revocation_granularity_seq`, and `compatibility_matrix_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-objective-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable objective spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_objective_policy_hash`, `objective_weight_basis_hash`, `hysteresis_cutover_basis_hash`, `objective_weight_seq`, and `hysteresis_cutover_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-integrity-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable signal-integrity spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_integrity_policy_hash`, `objective_signal_basis_hash`, `lag_normalization_basis_hash`, `manipulation_verdict_basis_hash`, `objective_signal_seq`, `lag_normalization_seq`, and `manipulation_verdict_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable signal-federation spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_policy_hash`, `federation_attestation_basis_hash`, `observer_diversity_escrow_basis_hash`, `stale_feed_quarantine_basis_hash`, `signal_federation_seq`, `diversity_escrow_seq`, and `stale_feed_quarantine_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable signal-federation-rehabilitation spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_policy_hash`, `quarantine_release_basis_hash`, `escrow_unlock_basis_hash`, `clock_skew_compensation_basis_hash`, `quarantine_release_seq`, `escrow_unlock_seq`, and `clock_skew_compensation_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable coupling spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_policy_hash`, `selector_clamp_basis_hash`, `unlock_backpressure_basis_hash`, `skew_hysteresis_basis_hash`, `selector_clamp_seq`, `unlock_backpressure_seq`, and `skew_hysteresis_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable coupling-profile spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_policy_hash`, `coupling_profile_portfolio_basis_hash`, `coupling_family_upgrade_basis_hash`, `coupling_fallback_basis_hash`, `coupling_profile_portfolio_seq`, `coupling_family_upgrade_seq`, and `coupling_fallback_seq`.
- Every disclosure-lifecycle-signal-federation-rehabilitation-objective-coupling-profile-evidence-integrity-governed base artifact additionally carries immutable coupling-profile-evidence-integrity spine:
`attester_disclosure_lifecycle_signal_federation_rehabilitation_objective_coupling_profile_evidence_integrity_policy_hash`, `upgrade_signal_attestation_basis_hash`, `stale_signal_tolerance_basis_hash`, `anti_regression_proof_basis_hash`, `upgrade_signal_attestation_seq`, `stale_signal_tolerance_seq`, and `anti_regression_proof_seq`.

### Reclaimable Artifact Classes

1. `migration_projected_version`
- Artifact key: `projected_version_key`.
- Eligible only when version is dominated/compacted in migration metadata and is not effective lineage winner.
- Rehydration source: retained root lineage + path manifest + migration spec chain.

2. `interval_surgery_projection`
- Artifact key: `surgery_projection_key`.
- Eligible only when surgery record is superseded by higher precedence visible surgery for the same component.
- Rehydration source: retained surgery planning basis (`core_id`, `component_id`, `policy_seq`, `strategy_id`, `plan_tx_asof`, basis/policy/strategy hashes).

3. `claim_revision_payload`
- Artifact key: `revision_id`.
- Eligible only when `claim_revision_kernel_v1` remains visible and deterministic query semantics do not require payload-resident fields.
- Rehydration source: visible `BasePayloadCapsuleRecord` referenced by retention manifest.

4. `evidence_atom_payload`
- Artifact key: `evidence_id`.
- Eligible only when `evidence_atom_kernel_v1` remains visible and confidence aggregation inputs stay kernel-complete.
- Rehydration source: visible `BasePayloadCapsuleRecord` referenced by retention manifest.

5. `rule_record_payload`
- Artifact key: `rule_id`.
- Eligible only for non-executable rule payload surfaces.
- Rehydration source: visible `BasePayloadCapsuleRecord`; executable rule kernel fields are never reclaimable.

### Base Semantic-Kernel Profiles

Each base payload class has deterministic non-reclaimable kernel bytes and hash:
`semantic_kernel_hash = H(ns_skernel, artifact_class, gc_artifact_key, kernel_profile_id, kernel_bytes)`.

1. `claim_revision_kernel_v1`
- Required fields: `revision_id`, `core_id`, `valid_interval`, `assertion_kind`, `origin_tx_id`, `justification_policy`, and deterministic ranking fields.

2. `evidence_atom_kernel_v1`
- Required fields: `evidence_id`, `independence_key`, `source_reliability_bp`, `extraction_quality_bp`, and canonical source fingerprint.

3. `rule_record_kernel_v1`
- Required fields: `rule_id`, `rule_reliability_bp`, `rule_logic_hash`, `rule_dsl_ast_hash`, and rule version metadata required for deterministic inference execution.

### Retention Policy and Plan Canonicalization

For each `artifact_class`, active retention policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_retention_policies, key=(policy_seq DESC, retention_policy_id ASC)))`.

Policy payload fields:
- `artifact_class`
- `policy_seq`
- `proof_profile_id`
- `min_assert_age_ms`
- `retention_floor_tx_id`
- `policy_hash`

Proof profiles:
- `rehydratable_from_retained_lineage_v1`:
for derived projection classes; deterministic lineage/spec prerequisites must be visible and unpoisoned.
- `rehydratable_from_base_capsule_v1`:
for base payload classes; deterministic kernel hash must validate and matching `BasePayloadCapsuleRecord` must be visible.

For each base `artifact_class`, active capsule cache policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_cache_policies, key=(policy_seq DESC, capsule_cache_policy_id ASC)))`.

Cache policy payload fields:
- `artifact_class`
- `policy_seq`
- `cache_profile_id`
- `max_serve_latency_ms`
- `lease_floor_tx_span`
- `eviction_profile_id`
- `policy_hash`

Current cache profile family:
- `bounded_rehydrate_latency_v1`:
every visible reclaimed base key must reference a visible active warm lease for its `capsule_id`;
eviction is blocked while any such key remains visible reclaimed.

Current eviction profile family:
- `pin_if_reclaimed_v1`:
capsule eviction is admissible only when deterministic pinned reclaimed-key set is empty.

For each base `artifact_class`, active capsule profile policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_profile_policies, key=(policy_seq DESC, capsule_profile_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule profile policy payload fields:
- `artifact_class`
- `policy_seq`
- `write_codec_profile_id`
- `write_key_epoch_seq`
- `read_compat_tuples` (`sorted(tuple(codec_profile_id, key_epoch_seq))`)
- `rotation_profile_id`
- `policy_hash`

Current deterministic codec families:
- `base_payload_codec_v1` (identity canonical framing).
- `base_payload_codec_zstd_aead_v1` (deterministic chunk framing + deterministic AEAD wrapper metadata).

Current key profile family:
- `key_epoch_seq` monotonic integer per base `artifact_class`; higher values represent newer wrapping epochs.

Current rotation profile family:
- `deterministic_transcode_rewrap_v1`:
decode canonical payload bytes from source capsule tuple, verify payload hash, then deterministically encode/rewrap under target write tuple.

For each base `artifact_class`, active capsule tier policy family at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_tier_policy_families, key=(family_seq DESC, capsule_tier_policy_family_id ASC)))`.

Capsule tier family payload fields:
- `artifact_class`
- `family_seq`
- `selector_profile_id`
- `admissibility_profile_id`
- `cost_profile_id`
- `tier_defs` (`sorted(tuple(tier_id, tier_rank, cache_policy_hash, profile_policy_hash, max_warm_bytes, max_rotate_bytes, max_rehydrate_ops, overflow_rank))`)
- `policy_hash`

Current selector profile family:
- `reclaimed_key_predicate_chain_lex_v1`:
deterministic ordered predicate chain over canonical selector features
`(artifact_class, gc_artifact_key, capsule_id, reclaim_age_ms, pinned_key_count, payload_size_bytes, key_epoch_seq, codec_profile_id)`.

Current tier admissibility profile family:
- `tier_member_policy_integrity_v1`:
every tier must reference visible immutable cache/profile member policies and assignment tuples must satisfy selector predicate provenance and active tuple admissibility.

Current tier cost profile family:
- `deterministic_budgeted_placement_v1`:
for fixed candidate set, compute deterministic per-key
`utility_bp`, `predicted_warm_bytes`, `predicted_rotate_bytes`, `predicted_rehydrate_ops`;
place keys by tier rank then utility rank under explicit tier bounds with required overflow-tier fallback.

For each active `(artifact_class, family_seq)`, active capsule tier telemetry policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_tier_telemetry_policies, key=(telemetry_seq DESC, capsule_tier_telemetry_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule tier telemetry policy payload fields:
- `artifact_class`
- `family_seq`
- `telemetry_seq`
- `window_profile_id`
- `utility_profile_id`
- `anti_gaming_profile_id`
- `lookback_window_count`
- `policy_hash`

Current telemetry window profile family:
- `deterministic_hlc_window_chain_v1`:
telemetry is admitted only for closed canonical windows with
`telemetry_window_id = H(ns_ctwin, window_start_tx_id, window_end_tx_id)`.

Current utility profile family:
- `deterministic_capped_latency_savings_v1`:
`utility_bp = clamp_bp(w_hit * hit_rate_bp + w_latency * latency_saved_bp + w_reuse * reuse_bp - w_rotate * rotate_cost_bp - anti_gaming_penalty_bp)`,
where all terms and coefficients are deterministic integer basis-point transforms bound by `policy_hash`.

Current anti-gaming profile family:
- `observer_cap_and_diversity_guard_v1`:
per-observer utility mass is capped before aggregation, dominant observer share is bounded, and a distinct-observer floor is enforced before utility is admissible.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule global budget policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_global_budget_policies, key=(budget_seq DESC, capsule_global_budget_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule global budget policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `budget_seq`
- `fair_share_profile_id`
- `sla_profile_id`
- `max_global_warm_bytes`
- `max_global_rotate_bytes`
- `max_global_rehydrate_ops`
- `class_weight_tuples` (`sorted(tuple(artifact_class, fair_share_weight_bp, sla_floor_warm_bp, sla_floor_rotate_bp, sla_floor_rehydrate_bp, sla_tier_rank))`)
- `policy_hash`

Current global fair-share profile family:
- `weighted_floor_waterfill_v1`:
allocate deterministic class floors first, then distribute residual resource budgets by weighted utility pressure with lexicographic tie-break
`(sla_shortfall_bp DESC, utility_density_bp DESC, artifact_class ASC)`.

Current global SLA profile family:
- `strict_floor_then_utility_v1`:
SLA floor allocations are hard constraints before weighted utility distribution; lower-rank classes cannot consume residual budgets that would violate a higher-rank class floor.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule global budget memory policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_global_budget_memory_policies, key=(memory_policy_seq DESC, capsule_global_budget_memory_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule global budget memory policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `memory_policy_seq`
- `horizon_profile_id`
- `burst_decay_profile_id`
- `sla_debt_profile_id`
- `deficit_cap_bp`
- `burst_credit_cap_bp`
- `sla_debt_cap_bp`
- `deficit_decay_ratio_bp`
- `burst_decay_ratio_bp`
- `sla_debt_decay_ratio_bp`
- `policy_hash`

Current long-horizon carryover profile family:
- `deterministic_deficit_carryover_v1`:
`deficit_carry_next_bp = min(deficit_cap_bp, floor(deficit_carry_prev_bp * deficit_decay_ratio_bp / 10000) + unmet_fair_share_bp)`.

Current burst-credit profile family:
- `deterministic_burst_credit_decay_v1`:
`burst_credit_next_bp = min(burst_credit_cap_bp, floor(burst_credit_prev_bp * burst_decay_ratio_bp / 10000) + underuse_share_bp - overuse_share_bp)`,
then clamp to `[0, burst_credit_cap_bp]`.

Current SLA debt rollback profile family:
- `deterministic_sla_debt_rollback_v1`:
`sla_debt_next_bp = min(sla_debt_cap_bp, floor(sla_debt_prev_bp * sla_debt_decay_ratio_bp / 10000) + sla_shortfall_bp - sla_repayment_bp)`,
where `sla_repayment_bp` is deterministically computed from allocation above floor under active arbitration and clamped to non-negative range.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule utilization attestation policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_utilization_attestation_policies, key=(utilization_policy_seq DESC, capsule_utilization_attestation_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule utilization attestation policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `utilization_policy_seq`
- `window_profile_id`
- `attester_quorum_profile_id`
- `reconciliation_profile_id`
- `close_lag_windows`
- `reconcile_grace_windows`
- `max_reconcile_delta_bp`
- `policy_hash`

Current utilization window profile family:
- `deterministic_hlc_budget_window_chain_v1`:
window boundaries are derived only from canonical HLC buckets and serialized as
`utilization_window_id = H(ns_cguwin, window_start_tx_id, window_end_tx_id)`.

Current attester quorum profile family:
- `deterministic_attester_quorum_tiered_cutover_v1`:
admissible attesters are sorted by canonical `attester_id`,
effective signer role (`legacy`/`new`) is resolved from active rotation lineage,
new-set quorum requires deterministic trust-tier weighted threshold,
and cutover windows additionally require deterministic overlap/continuity threshold before signatures are accepted.

Current late reconciliation profile family:
- `deterministic_late_window_carryforward_v1`:
late deltas may adjust only windows within `reconcile_grace_windows` and must carry-forward corrections into one deterministic `carry_forward_window_id` per class/window.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule attester trust policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_attester_trust_policies, key=(attester_policy_seq DESC, capsule_attester_trust_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule attester trust policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `attester_policy_seq`
- `identity_profile_id`
- `trust_tier_profile_id`
- `cutover_profile_id`
- `continuity_profile_id`
- `dual_quorum_windows`
- `min_overlap_attesters`
- `min_cutover_trust_weight_bp`
- `policy_hash`

Current attester identity profile family:
- `deterministic_attester_identity_binding_v1`:
each attester is bound to canonical `(attester_id, principal_id, key_epoch_seq)` tuples with deterministic alias collapse and key-epoch monotonicity checks.

Current attester trust-tier profile family:
- `deterministic_trust_tier_weighted_quorum_v1`:
quorum mass is deterministic sum of `quorum_weight_bp` over admissible signatures grouped by trust tier, with fixed tie-break and no runtime-local weighting.

Current attester cutover profile family:
- `deterministic_dual_quorum_cutover_v1`:
for windows inside configured cutover horizon, attestation must satisfy both new-set weighted quorum and legacy overlap/continuity threshold;
outside horizon, only new-set weighted quorum is required.

Current attester continuity profile family:
- `deterministic_historical_proof_chain_v1`:
retired attester/key proofs remain valid only through admitted continuity records linking legacy and successor identities/keys with canonical proof hashes.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule attester accountability policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_attester_accountability_policies, key=(accountability_policy_seq DESC, capsule_attester_accountability_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule attester accountability policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `accountability_policy_seq`
- `fault_profile_id`
- `slash_profile_id`
- `decay_profile_id`
- `reinstatement_profile_id`
- `max_slash_bp_per_window`
- `slash_debt_cap_bp`
- `decay_recovery_bp_per_window`
- `probation_min_windows`
- `min_reinstatement_trust_weight_bp`
- `policy_hash`

Current attester fault profile family:
- `deterministic_attester_fault_catalog_v1`:
fault evidence rows are canonicalized by `(fault_event_id, attester_id, fault_class_id, evidence_hash)` with per-window dedupe and immutable class severities.

Current attester slash/decay profile family:
- `deterministic_attester_slash_decay_v1`:
slash debt and reputation updates are computed by fixed integer basis-point equations with deterministic clamping and no runtime-local penalties.

Current attester reinstatement profile family:
- `deterministic_attester_probation_reinstate_v1`:
reinstatement requires deterministic probation-window completion, bounded debt recovery, and minimum recovered trust-weight evidence.

Capsule attester-set rotation planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester trust policy.
- Enumerate canonical attester roster candidates and key epochs; sort by `(attester_id ASC, key_epoch_seq ASC)`.
- Classify each candidate as `legacy`/`new`/`retired` according to deterministic cutover schedule anchored to `utilization_window_id`.
- Compute:
`attester_set_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, trust_tier_id, quorum_weight_bp, key_epoch_seq, set_role, roster_proof_hash)))`.
- For each roster tuple compute:
`rotation_projection_key = H(ns_cgatrotpk, budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, attester_id, attester_policy_seq, rotation_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_set_rotation_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_set_basis_hash`.

Capsule attester continuity planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, continuity_epoch_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester trust policy plus latest visible rotation lineage.
- Enumerate deterministic legacy->successor continuity candidates (identity aliases and key-epoch transitions) sorted by `(legacy_attester_id ASC, successor_attester_id ASC, legacy_key_epoch_seq ASC, successor_key_epoch_seq ASC)`.
- Verify canonical continuity proof envelopes and compute:
`attester_continuity_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatcbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_epoch_id, sorted(tuple(legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, legacy_key_epoch_seq, successor_key_epoch_seq, continuity_proof_hash)))`.
- For each candidate compute:
`continuity_projection_key = H(ns_cgatcontpk, budget_domain_id, continuity_epoch_id, legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_continuity_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_continuity_basis_hash`.

Capsule attester accountability planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester trust policy + active attester accountability policy + active attester adjudication policy + effective attester rotation/continuity/adjudication/appeal lineage.
- Enumerate canonical finalized adjudication rows for target window and participating attesters; sort by `(attester_id ASC, dispute_id ASC, fault_event_id ASC, fault_class_id ASC)`.
- Resolve prior effective accountability tuple for each attester by precedence:
`(utilization_window_id DESC, accountability_seq DESC, tx_id DESC, capsule_attester_accountability_id ASC)`, else zero tuple.
- Compute:
`accountability_basis_hash = H(ns_cgaabasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, dispute_id, fault_event_id, fault_class_id, severity_bp, evidence_hash, rotation_projection_key, continuity_projection_key, adjudication_projection_key, appeal_projection_key)))`.
- For each attester compute deterministic next tuple:
`(slash_bp, slash_debt_bp, reputation_bp, probation_until_window_id)` and
`accountability_projection_key = H(ns_cgaaccpk, budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq, accountability_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_accountability_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_accountability_policy_hash`, `accountability_basis_hash`.

For each `budget_domain_id`, active capsule attester adjudication policy at `tx_asof` is:
`first(sort(visible_capsule_attester_adjudication_policies, key=(adjudication_policy_seq DESC, capsule_attester_adjudication_policy_id ASC)))`.

Capsule attester adjudication policy payload fields:
- `budget_domain_id`
- `adjudication_policy_seq`
- `jurisdiction_profile_id`
- `verdict_profile_id`
- `appeal_profile_id`
- `finality_profile_id`
- `min_jurisdiction_count`
- `min_weighted_verdict_bp`
- `appeal_window_windows`
- `max_appeal_rounds`
- `finality_delay_windows`
- `policy_hash`

Current adjudication profile families:
- `deterministic_attester_jurisdiction_quorum_v1`:
cross-domain jurisdiction tuples are canonicalized by `(jurisdiction_id, adjudicator_id, quorum_weight_bp)` with deterministic weighted-threshold checks.
- `deterministic_attester_verdict_ledger_v1`:
fault verdict tuples are canonicalized by `(dispute_id, fault_event_id, verdict_code, evidence_root_hash)` with immutable evidence-root lineage checks.
- `deterministic_attester_appeal_finality_v1`:
appeal rounds follow deterministic monotonic sequencing with bounded round counts and delayed finality closure.

Capsule attester fault adjudication planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester adjudication policy + active attester trust/accountability policies + effective rotation/continuity lineage.
- Enumerate canonical dispute rows and admitted cross-domain adjudicator verdict envelopes sorted by `(dispute_id ASC, attester_id ASC, jurisdiction_id ASC, adjudicator_id ASC)`.
- Validate deterministic jurisdiction quorum and weighted verdict thresholds under active adjudication profiles.
- Compute:
`adjudication_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, fault_event_id, jurisdiction_id, adjudicator_id, verdict_code, verdict_weight_bp, evidence_root_hash, verdict_envelope_hash)))`.
- For each resolved dispute tuple compute:
`adjudication_projection_key = H(ns_cgajadjpk, budget_domain_id, dispute_id, attester_id, adjudication_policy_seq, adjudication_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_fault_adjudication_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_adjudication_policy_hash`, `adjudication_basis_hash`.

Capsule attester appeal finality planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester adjudication policy + latest visible adjudication lineage for target disputes.
- Enumerate deterministic appeal candidates sorted by `(dispute_id ASC, attester_id ASC, appeal_round ASC)`.
- Validate appeal-round monotonicity, appeal window bounds, and finality delay bounds.
- Compute:
`appeal_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajappbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, appeal_round, appeal_evidence_hash, prior_adjudication_projection_key)))`.
- For each appeal/finality tuple compute:
`appeal_projection_key = H(ns_cgajapppk, budget_domain_id, dispute_id, attester_id, adjudication_policy_seq, appeal_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_appeal_finality_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_adjudication_policy_hash`, `appeal_basis_hash`.

Capsule attester reinstatement planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active attester trust policy + active attester accountability policy + latest visible accountability lineage.
- Enumerate probation-complete candidates sorted by `(attester_id ASC, probation_until_window_id ASC)`.
- Validate deterministic reinstatement gates:
minimum probation windows elapsed, slash debt below threshold, recovered trust weight above `min_reinstatement_trust_weight_bp`.
- Compute:
`reinstatement_basis_hash = H(ns_cgareinbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, slash_debt_bp, reputation_bp, recovered_trust_weight_bp, eligibility_hash)))`.
- For each reinstated attester compute:
`reinstatement_projection_key = H(ns_cgareinpk, budget_domain_id, attester_id, accountability_policy_seq, reinstatement_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_attester_reinstatement_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_accountability_policy_hash`, `reinstatement_basis_hash`.

Capsule global utilization attestation planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active global budget policy + active utilization attestation policy + active attester trust policy + active attester accountability policy + active attester adjudication policy.
- Resolve effective attester-set rotation for target `utilization_window_id` and applicable continuity lineage.
- Resolve effective accountability/reinstatement lineage for attesters participating in target window.
- Resolve effective adjudication/appeal-finality lineage for participating disputes and attesters in target window.
- Confirm target window is closed under `close_lag_windows`; open windows are inadmissible.
- Enumerate canonical utilization evidence rows for participating `artifact_class` entries and deterministic attester proofs; sort by `(artifact_class ASC, attester_id ASC, evidence_row_id ASC)`.
- Aggregate per-class used resources:
`(used_warm_bytes, used_rotate_bytes, used_rehydrate_ops)` with deterministic integer summation and dedupe.
- Compute:
`utilization_basis_hash = H(ns_cgubasis, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(artifact_class, attester_id, used_warm_bytes, used_rotate_bytes, used_rehydrate_ops, evidence_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`attester_set_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, trust_tier_id, quorum_weight_bp, key_epoch_seq, set_role, roster_proof_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`attester_continuity_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatcbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_epoch_id, sorted(tuple(legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, legacy_key_epoch_seq, successor_key_epoch_seq, continuity_proof_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`accountability_basis_hash = H(ns_cgaabasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, dispute_id, fault_event_id, fault_class_id, severity_bp, evidence_hash, rotation_projection_key, continuity_projection_key, adjudication_projection_key, appeal_projection_key)))`.
- Compute:
`adjudication_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, fault_event_id, jurisdiction_id, adjudicator_id, verdict_code, verdict_weight_bp, evidence_root_hash, verdict_envelope_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`appeal_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajappbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, appeal_round, appeal_evidence_hash, prior_adjudication_projection_key)))`.
- Compute:
`reinstatement_basis_hash = H(ns_cgareinbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, slash_debt_bp, reputation_bp, recovered_trust_weight_bp, eligibility_hash)))`.
- For each class compute:
`attestation_projection_key = H(ns_cguapk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, attestation_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_utilization_attestation_plan` with sorted class tuples and hashes:
`utilization_attestation_policy_hash`, `utilization_basis_hash`, `attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_set_basis_hash`, `attester_continuity_basis_hash`, `attester_accountability_policy_hash`, `accountability_basis_hash`, `reinstatement_basis_hash`, `attester_adjudication_policy_hash`, `adjudication_basis_hash`, `appeal_basis_hash`.

Capsule global utilization reconciliation planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, utilization_window_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active utilization attestation policy + active attester trust policy + active attester accountability policy + active attester adjudication policy and effective prior attestation tuple per class for target closed window.
- Resolve effective attester-set rotation and applicable continuity lineage for the target window.
- Resolve effective accountability/reinstatement lineage for attesters participating in the target window.
- Resolve effective adjudication/appeal-finality lineage for participating disputes and attesters in target window.
- Accept only late evidence rows whose source timestamps fall in target window and whose arrival occurs within `reconcile_grace_windows`.
- Compute deterministic per-class deltas:
`(delta_used_warm_bytes, delta_used_rotate_bytes, delta_used_rehydrate_ops)` with cap guard `max_reconcile_delta_bp`.
- Compute:
`reconciliation_basis_hash = H(ns_cgurecbasis, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(artifact_class, delta_used_warm_bytes, delta_used_rotate_bytes, delta_used_rehydrate_ops, late_evidence_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`attester_set_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, trust_tier_id, quorum_weight_bp, key_epoch_seq, set_role, roster_proof_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`attester_continuity_basis_hash = H(ns_cgatcbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, continuity_epoch_id, sorted(tuple(legacy_attester_id, successor_attester_id, legacy_key_epoch_seq, successor_key_epoch_seq, continuity_proof_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`accountability_basis_hash = H(ns_cgaabasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, dispute_id, fault_event_id, fault_class_id, severity_bp, evidence_hash, rotation_projection_key, continuity_projection_key, adjudication_projection_key, appeal_projection_key)))`.
- Compute:
`adjudication_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, fault_event_id, jurisdiction_id, adjudicator_id, verdict_code, verdict_weight_bp, evidence_root_hash, verdict_envelope_hash)))`.
- Compute:
`appeal_basis_hash = H(ns_cgajappbasis, budget_domain_id, adjudication_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(dispute_id, attester_id, appeal_round, appeal_evidence_hash, prior_adjudication_projection_key)))`.
- Compute:
`reinstatement_basis_hash = H(ns_cgareinbasis, budget_domain_id, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, sorted(tuple(attester_id, slash_debt_bp, reputation_bp, recovered_trust_weight_bp, eligibility_hash)))`.
- For each class compute deterministic carry-forward target:
`carry_forward_window_id = next_canonical_window(utilization_window_id)` and
`reconciliation_projection_key = H(ns_cgurecpk, budget_domain_id, artifact_class, budget_seq, utilization_policy_seq, utilization_window_id, reconciliation_seq)`.
- Emit immutable `capsule_utilization_reconciliation_plan` with sorted tuples and hashes:
`utilization_attestation_policy_hash`, `reconciliation_basis_hash`, `attester_trust_policy_hash`, `attester_set_basis_hash`, `attester_continuity_basis_hash`, `attester_accountability_policy_hash`, `accountability_basis_hash`, `reinstatement_basis_hash`, `attester_adjudication_policy_hash`, `adjudication_basis_hash`, `appeal_basis_hash`.

Capsule global budget memory planning snapshot for fixed `(budget_domain_id, tx_asof)`:
- Resolve active global budget policy + active global budget memory policy + active utilization attestation policy + active attester trust policy + active attester accountability policy + active attester adjudication policy.
- Enumerate participating base `artifact_class` entries sorted by `artifact_class ASC`.
- For each class resolve previous effective memory tuple by precedence:
`(budget_seq DESC, memory_seq DESC, tx_id DESC, capsule_global_budget_memory_id ASC)`, else zero tuple.
- For each class resolve effective utilization tuple from latest visible attestation plus latest visible reconciliation for each eligible closed window by precedence:
`(utilization_window_id DESC, attestation_seq DESC, reconciliation_seq DESC, tx_id DESC, record_id ASC)`.
- For each class derive deterministic transition inputs from visible prior arbitration and effective attested/reconciled utilization:
`(fair_share_target_bp, allocated_share_bp, used_share_bp, used_share_reconciled_bp, sla_shortfall_bp, allocated_above_floor_bp, reconciliation_delta_bp)`.
- Compute:
`global_budget_memory_basis_hash = H(ns_cgbmbasis, budget_domain_id, budget_seq, memory_policy_seq, utilization_policy_seq, attester_policy_seq, accountability_policy_seq, adjudication_policy_seq, tx_asof, sorted(tuple(artifact_class, deficit_carry_prev_bp, burst_credit_prev_bp, sla_debt_prev_bp, fair_share_target_bp, allocated_share_bp, used_share_bp, used_share_reconciled_bp, reconciliation_delta_bp, sla_shortfall_bp, allocated_above_floor_bp, attester_set_basis_hash, attester_continuity_basis_hash, accountability_basis_hash, reinstatement_basis_hash, adjudication_basis_hash, appeal_basis_hash)))`.
- Compute utilization lineage hash:
